Title: Data Analyst
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Coalesce Management Consultancy (CMC) have a consulting assignment available for a Senior Data Analyst for Change Signal/Predictive Insights for a client of ours in the Wind Energy Sector.




CMC provide expert engineering solutions on an international capacity for Advanced Engineering projects. Established in 2016, we set out our mission to be the best service-driven consulting business in global STEM markets, and after rapid growth in just a few years, we’re already delivering true expertise to major technology brands and leading companies around the world.




As we expand our reach and take on new projects, we have assignments available for experienced professionals to join our consultancy and deliver the expert solutions and high level of service we’re known for across multiple ventures.




By partnering with CMC, you get the chance to work with some of the world’s leading technology specialists. From our global offices we support you with each new project, providing a complete scope of work definition ahead of any engagement, so you know exactly what’s required by the assignment, before you land on-site. As the project is completed, we can continue to work with you by setting up your next project from our global network of leading technology companies.




Join our latest project:




We are looking for someone with:




Strong experience analysing large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and forward-looking insights
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations for engineering and business teams
Experience with predictive analytics or forecasting (identifying future changes, risks, or opportunities)
Background working in engineering, manufacturing, or industrial environments (wind experience highly desirable)
Proven ability to communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders and influence decision-making
Experience working closely with product or engineering teams to improve development processes through data
Senior-level experience with a hands-on, proactive approach and ability to work independently
Comfortable operating in ambiguous environments and helping shape new data-driven capabilities







If you have the relevant capability and are interested in pursuing this assignment, talk to our sales consultants today. finlay.gethin@cmcexpertisedelivered.com







Get in touch




Learn more about Coalesce Management Consulting, our projects and the support we provide to our consultants at https://expertisedelivered.com




No terminology in this advert is intended to discriminate on the grounds of age or experience, and we confirm that we are happy to accept applications from persons of any age or experience for this role.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.

This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.

In This Role, You Will


Receive and scope data requests from PR and local media, then deliver clean, press-ready datasets.
Work with PR and communications partners to shape data angles and check feasibility.
Build and maintain automated SQL/Python pipelines and templates for recurring requests.
Create and maintain self‑service dashboards and lightweight tools (e.g., Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau) so teams can quickly explore trends.
Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.


Please Apply If You Have


A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
Familiarity with visualization tools (Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau, or similar).
Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.


Nice-to-have


Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.
Experience working with marketing, search, or travel data.
Experience building lightweight self-serve dashboards.


Benefits And Perks


Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year
Focus on mental health and well-being:
Company-paid therapy sessions through SpringHealth
Company-paid subscription to HeadSpace
Company-wide week off a year - the whole team fully recharges (and returns without a pile-up of work!)
No meeting Fridays
Paid parental leave
6 weeks paid vacation + a day off for your birthday
Paid volunteer time
Focus on your career growth:
Development Dollars
Leadership development
Access to thousands of on-demand e-learnings
Travel Discounts
Employee Resource Groups
Pension plan contributions
Private health & life insurance
Lunch provided five days per week & breakfast once a week
Professional espresso machine
Awesome office in the city centre of Copenhagen (Rådhuspladsen)
Monthly social events, yoga, Thursday bars

Inclusion

At KAYAK, we want everyone to have the space to grow, share ideas and do great work. That’s why we’re focused on hiring the best talent from all walks of life and experiences, supporting them well and making sure no one feels like they have to fit a mold to belong here.

If you need accommodations during the application or interview process, or on the job, we’re here to support you. Please reach out to your recruiter to request any accommodations.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Hedehusene, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Req Number: 111959

Time Type: Full Time

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who brings structure and clarity to how data is understood, aligned, and connected across an Enterprise Data Platform. You take ownership of how business concepts, source data, and platform models are mapped end-to-end, ensuring consistency, reuse, and clarity across domains and products.

This role is for a hands-on specialist who raises the bar on data analysis quality, documentation, collaboration, and who remains effective in a fast-moving, complex delivery environment where priorities evolve. Working at the core of major digital programs, you ensure analytical work is rigorous, aligned, and fit for reuse – driving value sprint by sprint.

Sounds like you? If so – join us and help set the standard for how data supports real enterprise impact.

About Your Role

Anchored in Global IT, Enterprise Business Development drives some of the most transformative digital initiatives in DSV – connecting data, intelligence, and workflows across divisions, systems, and geographies. In this role, you will work closely with Domain Squad Leads, architects, and Chapter Leads to translate needs into aligned, cross-domain solutions. You ensure capabilities are scalable, interoperable, and built for the long term – while delivering tangible value in every sprint.

Key Responsibilities:



Support squads in complex data analysis and stakeholder situations, helping resolve ambiguities in source data, definitions, and ownership.
Strengthen collaboration and ways of working between business, data, and engineering teams by acting as a bridge between business meaning and technical implementation.
Validate and challenge data requirements and mappings to ensure alignment with the enterprise data platform vision, data mesh principles, and overall company strategy.
Collaborate closely with solution architects, information architects, subject matter experts, business analysts, and squad leads to establish strong feedback loops that ensure end-to-end coherence, data quality, and semantic consistency.
Perform in-depth data profiling and analysis to uncover patterns, anomalies, and inconsistencies in data.
Develop and execute data validation and verification processes to document and improve data integrity and quality.
Create detailed reports and visualizations to communicate data profiling results and insights.



Qualifications

You are a trusted Data Analyst with strong analytical skills and the ability to navigate competing priorities in a complex, global, and matrix organisation. You are skilled at engaging stakeholders across both business and technical communities. You bring experience from large-scale agile delivery environments, you are comfortable supporting multiple squads in parallel, and you excel at translating business needs into clear, actionable requirements that enable scalable solutions.

To thrive and succeed in this role, you have:



2-5 years of experience in roles such as Mapping Analyst, Data Analyst, Data Scientist or similar senior positions.
Proven ability to engage business and data stakeholders to capture business definitions, source data semantics, and transformation logic, and translate them into precise and usable data insights.
Experience working closely with data engineers, solution architects, information architects, and product owners in data platform or analytics environments.
Good knowledge of data platforms, data warehousing or lakehouse concepts, business intelligence, and data transformation patterns. Exposure to logistics, supply chain, or transport domains is beneficial, but not required.
Experience with data profiling tools (e.g. Databricks, or similar).
Familiarity with data quality frameworks and concepts such as data cleansing, data governance, and master data management.
Knowledge of programming languages like Python or R for data analysis (preferred).
Knowledge of cloud systems (e.g. AWS or Azure) and experience in software development is an advantage.



Ready to take the next step?

We accept applications until 30th April 2026, but we screen candidates on an ongoing basis – so we recommend applying as early as possible. No cover letter is needed; just include a brief motivation in your CV.

If you would like to know more about the role, you are welcome to contact Senior Strategy Manager, Catalin Popescu, on constantin.c.popescu@dsv.com

DSV – Global transport and logistics

Working at DSV means playing in a different league.

As a global leader in transport and logistics, we have been on an extraordinary journey of growth. Let’s grow together as we continue to innovate, digitalise and build on our achievements.

With close to 150,000 colleagues in over 90 countries, we work every day to offer solid services and meet our customers’ needs and help them achieve their goals. We know that the best way to achieve this is by bringing in new talent, fresh perspectives and ambitious individuals like you.

At DSV, performance is in our DNA. We don’t just work – we aim to shape the future of logistics. This ambition fuels a dynamic environment built on collaboration with world-class team players, accountability and action. We value inclusivity, embrace different cultures and respect the dignity and rights of every individual. If you want to make an impact, be trusted by customers and grow your career in a forward-thinking company – this is the place to be.

Start here. Go anywhere

Visit dsv.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Måløv, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about transforming data into meaningful insights that support smarter decision‑making? As our new Data Analyst, you will build user‑centric data solutions, collaborate closely with stakeholders, and help strengthen how data is used across our Quality Control (QC) organisation. This is a great opportunity to make a real impact in an environment that values initiative, curiosity and open dialogue.

Your new role

In this role, you will work hands‑on with data to design, build and maintain analytics solutions that support daily operations. You will partner closely with end users to understand needs, test solutions and continuously refine your work.

Your Main Responsibilities Will Include


Building and maintaining data pipelines that ensure reliable data flows
Developing intuitive Power BI dashboards and impactful visual reporting
Working with SQL to extract, structure and analyse data
Contributing to Power Platform solutions, Python scripts and data‑modelling activities
Conducting user interviews, running tests and iteratively improving solutions


Your new department

You will become part of a team that values collaboration, constructive discussions and diverse perspectives. The environment is dynamic, solution‑oriented and user‑focused, where people are encouraged to challenge ideas, explore new approaches and take ownership of their work. Based in our Måløv site, you will work closely with colleagues across QC and contribute to shaping the digital tools that support their daily work.

Your Skills And Qualifications

We are looking for someone who enjoys working with data and building solutions that truly help users. You thrive in a role where you combine technical skills with stakeholder collaboration.

To Succeed In This Role, You Bring


A relevant bachelor’s or master’s degree in data analytics, data science or a similar field
Hands‑on experience developing insightful dashboards using Power BI or similar visualisation tools
Solid experience working with SQL, turning raw data into clear, actionable insights
Strong proficiency in spoken and written English, enabling effective collaboration with global stakeholders
Experience or curiosity around Python for data analysis, modelling.
Experience testing PoCs and deploying solutions from protypes to production.
Experience with Git versioning and agile ways of working.


You are proactive and self‑driven, with a strong sense of ownership in your work. You enjoy collaborating with stakeholders, asking insightful questions, exploring different perspectives and navigating changing priorities.

Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

There is, of course, more on offer here than the uniqueness of our culture and the extraordinary results we produce. Being part of a global healthcare company means opportunities to learn and develop are all around us, while our benefits are designed with your career and life stage in mind.

Contact

For further information about the position, please contact Senior Manager Tilde Schwaner at +45 30 79 42 09. Please note that Tilde is available on working days between 08:30 and 09:00.

Application Deadline

15th of April

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The power grid is changing faster than the software running it. Renewables are flooding supply, prices swing wildly, and millions of EV batteries sit idle on driveways every night, completely disconnected from the markets that need them most. Enkel is the platform that closes that gap. We integrate directly with energy markets and connect distributed EV assets to the grid in real time - turning idle batteries into a network. For consumers, that means lower bills and earnings from their car. For grid operators, it means distributed flexibility at scale, without building a single new power plant. We are a team with deep roots in energy markets, software, engineering and mathematics. Based in Copenhagen, we are building the connection between consumers and the grid: a layer that every stakeholder in the energy transition needs.




We are looking for a data engineer who wants their work to matter. Someone who cares deeply about reliability and performance because the systems they build operate in real markets, in real time.

You likely have 3+ years of relevant experience building and operating production pipelines and data systems. You’re ready to take on problems with real technical and commercial impact. You are strong in Python, comfortable working with streaming or event-driven infrastructure, and know how to build systems that remain reliable under pressure. Experience with Rust or C++ is a plus, but not a requirement. Importantly, you want ownership, not a ticket queue.




What You Will Do




Build and evolve the data infrastructure at the heart of our optimisation platform, ingesting energy market price feeds, EV charger telemetry, and grid signals across latency profiles
Develop the backtesting and simulation framework used daily to validate optimisation and dispatch strategies
Improve latency and reliability across the stack, directly affecting the quality and speed of decision-making in live markets
Scale the platform to support 10x asset growth while maintaining performance, resilience, and operational simplicity
Work closely with software engineers, optimisation specialists, and the wider team to ensure models are built on trusted, production-grade data systems




What We Offer




A mission worth showing up for – and a company with the traction to prove it is working
Real ownership from day one, alongside a high-calibre highly technical team that values clear thinking, fast execution, and big ambition
Fast pace and decision-making speed, without sacrificing engineering rigour
Competitive compensation




We know great candidates come from many different paths and encourage applications from all backgrounds.




Even if you don’t meet every requirement, if you are excited about building the future of energy, we are excited to hear from you!

Experience with Rust or C++ is a plus, but not a requirement. Importantly, you want ownership, not a ticket queue.


What You Will Do


  • Build and evolve the data infrastructure at the heart of our optimisation platform, ingesting energy market price feeds, EV charger telemetry, and grid signals across latency profiles
  • Develop the backtesting and simulation framework used daily to validate optimisation and dispatch strategies
  • Improve latency and reliability across the stack, directly affecting the quality and speed of decision-making in live markets
  • Scale the platform to support 10x asset growth while maintaining performance, resilience, and operational simplicity
  • Work closely with software engineers, optimisation specialists, and the wider team to ensure models are built on trusted, production-grade data systems


What We Offer


  • A mission worth showing up for – and a company with the traction to prove it is working
  • Real ownership from day one, alongside a high-calibre highly technical team that values clear thinking, fast execution, and big ambition
  • Fast pace and decision-making speed, without sacrificing engineering rigour
  • Competitive compensation


We know great candidates come from many different paths and encourage applications from all backgrounds.


Even if you don’t meet every requirement, if you are excited about building the future of energy, we are excited to hear from you!

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Analytics & Insights
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

The banking industry is rapidly evolving, and Danske Bank is leading this transformation. Join a major bank in the region and contribute to our commercial agenda. If you are an experienced Data Analyst with strong technical expertise, ready to leverage your skills, we have a great opportunity for you!

About The Role

As a Senior Data Analyst you will be part of the Commercial Excellence Team within Danske Bank's Business Customer unit as part of the Nordic Analytic and Insights Team. In this role you will be part of delivering commercial data driven initiatives that stretch across all Nordic countries, having close dialogue with stakeholders on all levels of the bank’s Business Customers unit, a unit that serves +200 000 small and mid-sized corporates across market areas.

You can expect to solve complex problems, support decision-makers with data-driven recommendations, and become a trusted advisor for key stakeholders. You will work with senior leadership yet be comfortable in working effectively at all levels of large organisations. You will also need the ability to balance a holistic view with attention to detail.

Qualifications And Requirements

You have a curious mindset to challenge status quo and bring enthusiasm to the team with a can-do attitude. As a Data Analyst your main responsibilities will be:


Contribute to identifying and designing commercial activities
Identify leads for aligned activities
Provide business cases for commercial activities
Strategic analysis that supports our Forward 28 Strategy and commercial agenda
Reporting, tracking and proactive analytics
Ad hoc data requests


You are required to have:


Proficiency in English, with strong language and writing skills.
Proficiency in Python and SQL.
A commercial mindset with solid analytical skills and a structured, data-driven approach.
Strong communication skills.


It’s also nice if you have:


Experience in the Banking industry.
B2B understanding.
Experience working in an agile set-up with remote team members.


The ideal candidate possesses experience from specialised data teams, ideally within the financial industry, complemented by a technical background and demonstrated ability to translate complex analytics into clear, actionable insights. While not essential, having a keen interest in AI, frontrunner in implementing Danske Bank AI solutions and identifying business opportunities would be advantageous.

What We Offer

We offer a dynamic and supportive work environment where you can thrive both personally and professionally. You'll be part of a team of motivated and sociable colleagues who are committed to what they do, making for a fun and engaging workplace. We believe in empowering our employees by providing autonomy in their roles, allowing you to take initiative and make meaningful contributions. Additionally, we are committed to support a culture of continuous learning and development, ensuring you have the resources and opportunities to grow your career.

Interested?

We are seeking a candidate based in Denmark who will report to Louise Nørsten the Head of Analytics & Insights for Business Customers – please reach out to Louise if you have any clarifying questions:

lnrs@danskebank.dk. Please apply as soon as possible as we conduct interviews on an ongoing basis.

Title: Data Annotator
Place: Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Innodata (NASDAQ: INOD) is a leading data engineering company. With more than 2,000 customers and operations in 13 cities around the world, we are an AI technology solutions provider-of-choice for 4 out of 5 of the world’s biggest technology companies, as well as leading companies across financial services, insurance, technology, law, and medicine.

By combining advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence (ML/AI) technologies, a global workforce of subject matter experts, and a high-security infrastructure, we’re helping usher in the promise of AI. Innodata offers a powerful combination of both digital data solutions and easy-to-use, high-quality platforms.




Our global workforce includes over 5,000 employees in the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, the Philippines, India, Sri Lanka, Israel and Germany.




About the Role




Job Title: Data Annotator

Employment Type: Freelance / Project-Based

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Work Hours: Flexible; minimum commitment of 4–5 hours per day

Eligibility: Open to candidates with a Bachelor’s degree







We are seeking Data Annotator to support a data annotation and linguistic quality project aimed at training Large Language Models (LLMs). This is a freelance, remote opportunity that offers flexible working hours.

The role involves understanding cultural and linguistic nuances, reviewing and annotating data, and following detailed project-specific guidelines to enhance AI performance and accuracy.




Key Responsibilities




Perform data annotation, and text evaluation in accordance with project-specific guidelines.
Ensure high-quality, accurate, and contextually relevant annotations to support Large Language Model (LLM) training.
Review, validate, and refine AI-generated content for grammatical, semantic, and contextual accuracy.
Maintain consistency and quality across all deliverables while adhering to established project standards and timelines.
Collaborate effectively with the project team to provide feedback and contribute to continuous improvement.
Maintain strict confidentiality and comply with all project deadlines and quality assurance protocols.




Required Skills & Qualifications




Bachelor’s degree in mandatory.
Good proficiency in the English language
Prior experience in data annotation, linguistic evaluation, or AI training projects.




What Makes This Role Exciting




.Fully remote opportunity, allowing you to work comfortably from home.
Flexible working hours (4–5 hours per day) to suit your schedule.
The chance to contribute to cutting-edge AI and Large Language Model (LLM) training initiatives, shaping the future of language technology.




Note:

For this role, you are required to complete the English assessment within two (2) days from the date it is assigned to you.

Title: Data & AI Graduate
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Twoday er den førende digitale transformationspartner i Nordeuropa med en global tilstedeværelse. Med cirka 3.000 teknologer samarbejder vi med de mest beundrede private og offentlige organisationer for at levere banebrydende digitale løsninger. Vores dybe brancheekspertise omfatter Data & AI, softwareudvikling, digitale oplevelser og forretningsapplikationer.

Data & AI Graduate Program

Bliv Graduate hos Twoday Data & AI og kickstart din karriere som konsulent.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Som Graduate hos Twoday Data & AI kommer du igennem et skræddersyet program på 12 måneder med opstart 1. september, som forbereder dig til livet som konsulent hos Twoday Data & AI.

Du får relevant uddannelse, en udvalgt mentor, som følger dig gennem hele forløbet – og du får kontakt med vores kunder allerede fra dag ét.

Efter 12 måneder befinder du dig i en spændende hverdag med udfordrende projekter for vores kunder, hvoraf mange er blandt de største virksomheder i Danmark. Forløbet finder sted på vores kontorer både i Aarhus, Kolding, Aalborg og København.

Hvem er du?

Det vigtigste for os er, at du brænder for data og analyse, og drømmer om at være i en techvirksomhed. Det gør alt nemmere, når der er motivation bag interessen.

Vores Graduates inden for Data & AI har ofte en videregående uddannelse fra eksempelvis DTU, CBS, ITU, AAU eller KU. Det kan være cand.it, cand.polit., cand.merc.bi, HA(it.) eller anden relevant uddannelse.

Som konsulent skal du være god til at sætte dig i andres behov og forstå vores kunders forretning. Du skal være løsningsorienteret og desuden trives med en hverdag, som er langt fra triviel. Sådan er det nemlig i konsulentbranchen.

Hvad kan du vælge?

Hos Data & AI har vi forløb inden for 2 forskellige fagområder, som du kan vælge mellem

Data Platform

Forløbet er meget teknisk backend-orienteret, og du vil lære at arbejde med SQL, Python (PySpark) og DAX. Du bliver certificeret i: Azure Fundamentals, Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate og Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate.

Data Insights

Forløbet er med fokus på Data Storytelling og semantisk modellering, og du vil lære at arbejde med Power BI og Tabular Editor, så du bliver dygtig til DAX, visualisering og Power Query. Du bliver certificeret i Power BI Data Analyst Associate og Tabular Editor.

Kort om vores Data & AI spor

12 måneders forløb

Forløbet starter 1. september, men vi ansætter løbende.

Fællesskab med andre

Du starter sammen med andre Data & AI Graduates, men du vil også indgå i et større Graduate netværk i Twoday.

Mentorordning

Du får en mentor, som tager dig med ud til spændende kunder og bliver en fast sparringspartner i dagligdagen.

Lønnet?

Selvfølgelig! Du bliver ansat på lige fod, som dine andre kollegaer.

IT-område

Data Analytics, AI og Business Intelligence.

At arbejde med data og analyse i Twoday

I dag findes der næsten ikke en virksomhed, som ikke anser data som en af de mest værdifulde ressourcer. Som Graduate i vores Data & AI spor vil du befinde dig i hjertet af denne transformation, hvor du hjælper virksomheder med at udnytte deres data optimalt, så de kan træffe informerede beslutninger på baggrund af pålidelige virksomhedsdata.

Som Graduate vil du arbejde på tværs af forskellige projekter og brancher, hvilket giver dig en bred vifte af erfaringer. Du vil ofte samarbejde med andre konsulenter, IT-specialister og virksomhedens ledelse for at levere skræddersyede løsninger. Denne tværfaglige tilgang kræver både teknisk ekspertise og stærke samarbejdsevner.

Vi er flere gange blevet kåret til årets Microsoft Data & AI Partner i Danmark, så her får du kollegaer, som virkelig ved, hvad de laver – og de er selvfølgelig klar til at hjælpe dig.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Send din ansøgning i dag, de vil løbende blive læst.

Der vil løbende blive indkaldt til indledende samtaler, men opstart er først 1. september 2026. Du ansøger ved at klikke på knappen nedenfor. Alle ansøgninger behandles fortroligt.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er Ann Brahe-Orlandi, Talent Acquisition Manager, klar til at hjælpe. Hun glæder sig til at høre fra dig på ann.brahe-orlandi@twoday.com.

Title: Data Integration Analyst
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Join our team as a Data Integration Analyst Enhance marketing investment data by unifying insights across platforms.

Please note This is a 12-month cover role at Lead Professional level, based out of our vibrant LEGO Campus (Billund). Relocation assistance is not available for this position.


Core Responsibilities

Historical data migration and alignment





Manually remap, transform, and validate in-year and historical actuals from two legacy planning systems into a single planning platform.
Resolve structural, field-level, and granularity differences to align with the target data model; reconcile results at record and aggregate levels to ensure completeness and accuracy.




Analytical continuity





Standardize legacy and current data to the target architecture to enable consistent year-over-year reporting and trend analysis.
Maintain cross-system comparability by defining and applying clear mapping rules, hierarchies, and crosswalks for metrics and dimensions.




Documentation and auditability





Create and maintain audit-ready documentation of mapping logic, transformation rules, validation checks, and reconciliation outcomes.
Establish version-controlled artifacts (e.g., mapping tables, data dictionaries, SOPs) to support audit requirements and provide a durable reference for future integrations.




Collaboration and quality control





Partner with data engineering, reporting, finance, and business stakeholders to validate outputs, gather feedback, and align on acceptance criteria.
Proactively identify inconsistencies, perform root-cause analysis, and resolve issues in a structured, traceable manner; communicate status, risks, and impacts.




Play your part in our team succeeding

As a Data Integration Analyst, you oversee the migration of marketing investment data from various legacy systems to a unified platform. You take charge of remapping, validating, and reconciling records, addressing structural differences to ensure they fit the new architecture. With your attention to detail and strict quality controls, you guarantee data accuracy and consistency for reporting and analytics. Your efforts also facilitate knowledge transfer across teams, making a significant impact on how we work together.

Do you have what it takes?





Demonstrated experience in data migration, mapping, and reconciliation across disparate systems and data models.
Advanced Excel proficiency (nested formulas, lookups/INDEX‑MATCH, pivot tables) and working knowledge of BI/reporting tools (Power BI preferred) and semantic layer concepts.
Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to manage and quality‑check large, complex historical datasets.
Strong documentation and process-tracking skills, including creating audit-ready artifacts and maintaining version-controlled materials.
Experience in marketing investment, finance, or procurement data environments is an advantage




Preferred Tools And Technical Skills





Databricks (SQL, Python); builds auditable transformations and reconciliations in a bespoke cloud environment.
Strong SQL; ETL/ELT and orchestration (e.g., Databricks Jobs, ADF/Airflow) for repeatable migration pipelines.
Power BI/semantic models; advanced Excel/Power Query; Git-based version control; solid data modelling and data quality practices.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Student Assistants for Data Analysis and Risk Management
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about sustainability and risk management? And do you have a good understanding of how to take data-driven decisions? Then, apply now as a Student Assistant.




You will join the Sustainable Procurement, Real Estate and Third-party Risk team, a collaborative and diverse group of 15 colleagues. Together, we work on developing tools and processes that improve sustainability performance, ensure high-quality environmental data, and drive cross-organisational initiatives related to procurement, office leases, and workplace services.




We are looking for someone who enjoys working with data, is curious about systems and processes, and thrives in a team-oriented environment. You will report to the Head of Sustainable Procurement, Real Estate and Third-party Risk and work from our office in Lyngby, Denmark.




Your main tasks will include: 



Supporting onboarding, follow-up, and analysis of suppliers in relation to risk and ESG assessments using data and business intelligence tools
Developing reports and insights to communicate progress, key findings, and improvement areas to stakeholders
Collaborating with suppliers and internal stakeholders to support corrective action plans
Contributing to the integration of ESG considerations into procurement processes
Assisting with ad hoc projects across teams, providing you with broad, hands-on experience



Your profile




We envision you as a motivated bachelor or master student, ideally with at least 1.5 years remaining in your studies. Your prior exposure to similar tasks through relevant work experience or academic coursework will be beneficial, especially within supply chain and risk management.




As a person, we believe you have an open and curious nature and easily build strong professional relationships. Moreover, you thrive in collaborative environments, while being able to independently take ownership of assignments. Also, you leverage your analytical skills to tackle challenges and maintain a structured and methodical approach to work.




On top of that, you will



Demonstrate solid process and system understanding including the use of MS office systems like Excel and Power Point
Have an interest in data analysis and can work with structured and unstructured data
Have a structured and analytical mindset
Communicate clearly in English (Nordic languages are a plus, but not required)



What we offer



A relevant student job with real responsibility and learning opportunities
Hands-on experience in sustainability, procurement, and risk management
Insight into working with data and business intelligence tools in a professional setting
A supportive and inclusive team environment
A community that offers leisure activities and social events
Flexible working hours (contracted 15 hours per week), with understanding for exam periods. Opportunity to work from home.




 If you are keen to make an impact in our supply chain and grow your professional skills, apply now to join us on this journey!




Please submit your application as soon as possible and no later than 5 May 2026, as we review applications and invite candidates for interviews on an ongoing basis.




A place to work and so much more




At COWI, we work together with our customers to shape a sustainable and liveable world. We do it by applying our knowledge and curiosity – and sometimes even our courage – to create the solutions the world needs today to enable a better tomorrow. That is why we say no to fossil-based projects and aspire to have 100 per cent of our revenue come from activities that move our customers towards sustainability.




We value differences and development and cultivate an environment of belonging and having fun. Because that is what brings out the best in you, at work and at home.




With offices primarily located in Scandinavia, the UK, North America, Poland and India, we are currently 8,000 people who bring their expertise in engineering, architecture, energy and the environment into play.




Got more questions
If you have any questions about the role, feel free to contact Nanna Rosenvinge Vibede at nrvi@cowi.com.




Get to know us even better at our website, www.cowi.com, where you can learn more about our projects, our strategy, what we want to achieve and what life is like at COWI.




We do our utmost to ensure that our recruitment process is as fair and unbiased as possible. You can help us by not including a photo in your CV when you apply.




Diversity is fundamental to COWI, inclusion is key.




COWI provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, COWI complies with applicable state and local laws governing non-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

Title: Graduate Program Data Science & Analytics - Denmark
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?


Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.


Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:


You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
You are fluent in English and Danish.
You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
You have the legal right to work in Denmark (e.g., residency, visa).
You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

Apply Now!

Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity. Apply today and discover what's in it for you!

Please ensure both your CV and all answers in the application form are completed in English.

About Us

Are you interested in joining one of Europe's leading PayTech companies? Do you want to be part of a diverse team, collaborating with colleagues from across Europe? If so, you're in the right place!

About The Team

We are the result of merging Nexi, Nets, and Sia, with a presence in over 25 countries. With over 80 years of experience, we offer plenty of opportunities for personal and professional growth

At Nexi, we learn, adapt, and think like entrepreneurs. Discover more about our culture by visiting this page: https://www.nexigroup.com/en/people/life-at-nexi/overview/?utm_source=career-platform&utm_medium=job-openings&utm_content=about-the-team

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About the company

We are a fast-growing, VC-backed energy-tech scale-up on a mission to connect electric vehicles directly with power markets and make smart charging completely frictionless for consumers. Our fully automated, data-driven platform forecasts energy needs, tracks electricity prices and grid conditions in real time, and optimises charging to maximise savings and unlock new revenue streams for our customers.

You will join a small, highly technical team working at the intersection of real-time data, energy markets and EV charging.




The role

As a Data Engineer, you will own and evolve the data infrastructure at the core of our optimisation platform. You’ll work closely with software engineers and optimisation specialists to ensure models and products are built on trusted, production-grade data systems.

Key responsibilities:

Design, build and operate production data pipelines and data models for time-series and event-driven workloads
Ingest and process external data feeds (market prices, asset telemetry, grid signals) into our core data platform
Improve latency, reliability and observability across the stack to support fast decision-making in live markets
Develop and maintain backtesting and simulation frameworks for new strategies and product features
Contribute to scaling the platform as the number of connected assets and markets grows




Tech stack (current and planned)

Python as the primary language
Cloud data platform (GCP or similar), containerised services and modern CI/CD
Relational databases (e.g. PostgreSQL) and time-series databases (e.g. TimescaleDB or similar)
Streaming / event-driven infrastructure and real-time data processing (e.g. Kafka, Pub/Sub, Kinesis, or equivalent)

You don’t need experience with our exact tools, but you should be comfortable working in a modern cloud/data environment and picking up new technologies.







About you

We imagine you have:

3–6 years of experience as a Data Engineer or similar, working with production-grade data systems
Strong hands-on skills in Python and SQL
Experience building and operating data pipelines for time-series, streaming or event-driven use cases
Solid understanding of reliability, performance, monitoring and testing for data-intensive systems
Experience from energy, trading, fintech, or other real-time, high-stakes domains is a strong plus
Bonus: familiarity with a systems language (e.g. Rust or C++) or with statistical / ML tooling




You’ll thrive here if you:

Enjoy end-to-end ownership of your systems, from design to production
Are pragmatic, curious, and comfortable in a startup/scale-up environment with high autonomy
Want your work to have a direct impact on decarbonisation and the future of electric mobility




What we offer

Competitive salary (50,000 - 55,000 DKK/month) plus 5–15% performance bonus
High degree of autonomy and ownership over core parts of the data platform
Close collaboration with experienced engineers and domain experts in energy and optimisation
Central Copenhagen office, flexible hybrid setup, and an informal, international team culture

Title: Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team

Are you passionate about applying Data Science in real-world scenarios? Do you want to leverage mathematics, statistics, and programming to help our clients make better decisions? Are you driven by data and analysis, and eager to translate insights into actionable recommendations? Join Annalect’s Data Science Consultancy team!




About the Role

We are looking for a new colleague to join Annalect, Omnicom Media Group’s specialist unit for data-driven analytics and decision support systems. Our work includes analyses that quantify the impact of our clients’ marketing activities and provide the foundation for ongoing optimization. Annalect consists of 60 employees, with 20 dedicated to the Data Science Consultancy team. Our team utilizes mathematics, statistics, and econometrics to process large volumes of data, delivering business-oriented decision support. As a Data Science Consultant, you will be involved in the entire project lifecycle for our clients – from data collection and coding, to building and optimizing statistical models in R, and communicating results and recommendations to clients. You will work closely with a project lead and the team, receiving ongoing support and feedback. As you gain experience, you will have the opportunity to conduct analyses independently, manage client communications, and deliver your own projects.

Key Responsibilities:

Collect client data and extract data from media systems
Programming and data processing
Structure, code, and interpret data
Develop statistical models in R
Analyze results and prepare recommendations in close collaboration with a project lead
Prepare deliverables for clients in PowerPoint and dashboards
Maintain ongoing dialogue with clients and media agencies throughout the project
Lead your own projects




Your Profile

We imagine that you:

Hold a relevant academic degree (e.g., Cand.Merc. (MAT), Cand.polit. IMM, AEF, or similar)
Have basic experience with statistical models and methodologies
Possess strong analytical skills
Are proficient in R Studio (or other programming languages such as Python, SAS, VBA, SQL and are eager to further develop your skills in R Studio)
Are experienced in Excel and PowerPoint
Are interested in building an understanding of marketing, digital platforms, and the media landscape




What We Offer

We offer a position in an exciting company with many opportunities. You will be part of a strong professional community and work alongside some of the industry’s most talented specialists. We also have a vibrant social environment, including Friday bars, annual parties, company football team, DHL race, and more. Our culture is relaxed, with room for fun and focus for deep work on projects.




Interested?

We look forward to receiving your application and CV as soon as possible. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting once the right candidate has been found.

If you have any questions about the position or the company, please contact Analytics Director Anders Mousten at anders.mousten@omc.com.

Title: Digital Analytics & Insights Specialist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Turn customer insights into commercial impact — and shape the future of digital retail with one of the biggest retailers in the Nordics

THE OPPORTUNITY

Join the strongest Commercial Business Team in the Nordic retail industry — and help shape how we understand, engage, and convert customers in a fast-moving digital landscape.

We are looking for a strategic, data-driven talent to turn insights into actions and elevate our commercial performance. As our new Digital Analytics & Insights Specialist, you will play a key role in shaping the future of pre-purchase analytics, uncovering trends and patterns, and translating them into clear, actionable insights.

You will be the funnel and conversion optimisation owner and lead the development and improvement of the on-site customer experience. You will work closely with a highly skilled, ambitious and collaborative commercial team that thrives on impact and shared success. If you’re motivated by data, excited by measurable outcomes and eager to build strong cross-functional partnerships - then this opportunity is for you.

What You Will Do


Build & enhance analytics frameworks: Define and build KPI dashboards to analyse funnel performance and pre-purchase behaviour.
Turn insights into action: Translate findings into clear commercial recommendations, improve conversion rates, and drive revenue through A/B testing and tag management.
Collaborate with experts: Partner with the post-purchase analytics team to connect insights across the entire customer journey.
Shape how we work with data: Challenge existing processes, propose new strategies, and influence how the organisation achieves data-driven commercial excellence



YOUR PROFILE

You bring a strong commercial mindset — you naturally translate insights into action and action into measurable business results. You are a curious, proactive, and confident communicator who thrives when collaborating across teams and influencing stakeholders.


2+ years of experience in e-commerce analytics with a deep interest in digital customer behaviour
Strong experience with GA4 and Google Tag Manager (GTM), especially in tracking, measurement and optimisation
A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field
Fluency in English and comfort working in an international environment
Bonus: experience with BigQuery, Looker Studio, or Qlik Sense



WHY JOIN US

Step into a role where your insights make a visible difference. You’ll own key parts of the digital funnel, shape conversion strategy, and influence decisions across marketing, product, and the wider commercial team.

If you’re ready to innovate, challenge, and leave a lasting impact, you’ll thrive here.

Apply today – we’re excited to learn more about you!

Note: Only applications submitted in English will be considered.

Applicants must have the legal right to work in Denmark at the time of employment. Please note that this position does not include visa sponsorship or relocation support.

RECRUITMENT PROCESS

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. The process may vary depending on the role, but generally follows these steps:


Assessments: PI (Predictive Index) Behavioral & Cognitive Assessments to better understand you and your drivers. You will receive these by email from our external partner once your application is submitted. If you have already completed them, you may skip this step.
Screening Call: Candidates who stand out in the initial review will be invited to a short online screening call.
Interviews: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to in-person interviews.



BOOZT PERKS


Great personal and internal career development opportunities
A culture built on our values of trust, freedom, and responsibility
Driven and passionate international colleagues
A generous employee discount
Healthy and delicious breakfast and lunch, top-quality coffee, and fresh fruit available all day
In-house gym, onsite masseuse, and medical doctor
An amazing HQ in central Copenhagen - the iconic, energy-efficient Krystallen building, recognized for its architectural design and sustainability. Enjoy breathtaking city views and much more!



A LITTLE BIT ABOUT US

We are one of the leading e-commerce players in the Nordics. We offer our customers fashion, kids, sports, beauty, and home on Boozt.com and Booztlet.com.

Our Boozt family consists of +1100 employees from more than 50+ nationalities; our diverse teams help us build an innovative and vibrant workplace.

Our headquarters are in the heart of Copenhagen at Krystallen (Kalvebod Brygge), supported by an office in Malmö and a data science hub in Aarhus. We drive innovation from our two tech offices in Vilnius, Lithuania, and Poznań, Poland, while our fully automated warehouse in Ängelholm is one of the world’s largest AutoStore systems!

We are an equal-opportunity employer that embraces diversity and inclusiveness.

­­­­­­­­­­­­­­­­­Would you like to join us on our exciting journey?

  • Build & enhance analytics frameworks: Define and build KPI dashboards to analyse funnel performance and pre-purchase behaviour.
  • Turn insights into action: Translate findings into clear commercial recommendations, improve conversion rates, and drive revenue through A/B testing and tag management.
  • Collaborate with experts: Partner with the post-purchase analytics team to connect insights across the entire customer journey.
  • Shape how we work with data: Challenge existing processes, propose new strategies, and influence how the organisation achieves data-driven commercial excellence


YOUR PROFILE

You bring a strong commercial mindset — you naturally translate insights into action and action into measurable business results. You are a curious, proactive, and confident communicator who thrives when collaborating across teams and influencing stakeholders.

  • 2+ years of experience in e-commerce analytics with a deep interest in digital customer behaviour
  • Strong experience with GA4 and Google Tag Manager (GTM), especially in tracking, measurement and optimisation
  • A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field
  • Fluency in English and comfort working in an international environment
  • Bonus: experience with BigQuery, Looker Studio, or Qlik Sense


WHY JOIN US

Step into a role where your insights make a visible difference. You’ll own key parts of the digital funnel, shape conversion strategy, and influence decisions across marketing, product, and the wider commercial team.

If you’re ready to innovate, challenge, and leave a lasting impact, you’ll thrive here.

Apply today – we’re excited to learn more about you!

Note: Only applications submitted in English will be considered.

Applicants must have the legal right to work in Denmark at the time of employment. Please note that this position does not include visa sponsorship or relocation support.

RECRUITMENT PROCESS

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. The process may vary depending on the role, but generally follows these steps:

  • Assessments: PI (Predictive Index) Behavioral & Cognitive Assessments to better understand you and your drivers. You will receive these by email from our external partner once your application is submitted. If you have already completed them, you may skip this step.
  • Screening Call: Candidates who stand out in the initial review will be invited to a short online screening call.
  • Interviews: Shortlisted candidates will be invited to in-person interviews.


BOOZT PERKS

  • Great personal and internal career development opportunities
  • A culture built on our values of trust, freedom, and responsibility
  • Driven and passionate international colleagues
  • A generous employee discount
  • Healthy and delicious breakfast and lunch, top-quality coffee, and fresh fruit available all day
  • In-house gym, onsite masseuse, and medical doctor
  • An amazing HQ in central Copenhagen - the iconic, energy-efficient Krystallen building, recognized for its architectural design and sustainability. Enjoy breathtaking city views and much more!


A LITTLE BIT ABOUT US

We are one of the leading e-commerce players in the Nordics. We offer our customers fashion, kids, sports, beauty, and home on Boozt.com and Booztlet.com.

Our Boozt family consists of +1100 employees from more than 50+ nationalities; our diverse teams help us build an innovative and vibrant workplace.

Our headquarters are in the heart of Copenhagen at Krystallen (Kalvebod Brygge), supported by an office in Malmö and a data science hub in Aarhus. We drive innovation from our two tech offices in Vilnius, Lithuania, and Poznań, Poland, while our fully automated warehouse in Ängelholm is one of the world’s largest AutoStore systems!

We are an equal-opportunity employer that embraces diversity and inclusiveness.

­­­­­­­­­­­­­­­­­Would you like to join us on our exciting journey?

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data Engineer




On behalf of our client we are recruiting a Data Engineer for a temp position, you will play a hands-on role in building, shaping, and operating data products that are reliable, well‑structured, and easy for stakeholders to consume. You will collaborate closely with local data teams, BI specialists, business partners, and platform teams to deliver scalable, source‑aligned data products and consumer‑ready datasets.

This role suits someone who enjoys creating long‑lasting solutions: robust pipelines, clear models, governance‑by‑design, and reusable patterns that elevate the overall data ecosystem.




Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain data pipelines and transformations using DBT, Snowflake, SQL, and Python.
Develop scalable, well‑structured data models to support BI, analytics, and data activation use cases.
Contribute to data product standards, including testing, documentation, lineage, and versioning.
Enable and accelerate local data teams through hands‑on coaching and platform guidance.
Collaborate with BI specialists and stakeholders to clarify requirements and translate them into usable data outputs and solution architectures.
Participate in design reviews and influence architectural decisions within the data engineering domain.




Contract Details

Duration: 9 months
Start: Latest 1 May
Location: Copenhagen — 3 days onsite per week

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Azure Data Engineer
Som Data Engineer hos cVation bygger du dataplatforme, datawarehouses og MLOps løsninger i skyen for vores kunder. Vi bruger teknologier som Databricks, Fabric, Spark, Azure Machine Learning, Data Factory og Azure Synapse. Vi søger profiler med erfaring inden for enten MLOps, datawarehouses eller dataplatforme, som vil bygge innovative dataløsninger.
cVation er et dansk udviklings- og konsulenthus, som bygger avancerede cloudløsninger på Azure med afsæt i markedets hurtigste og mest agile metoder. cVation blev stiftet i 2014, har modtaget tre Børsen Gazeller og vundet Microsofts Partner Award for App Innovation tre år i træk. Vi arbejder dedikeret med agile arbejdsmetoder, DevOps og Test Driven Development. Vi sætter en ære i at levere software af den højeste kvalitet, og tager desuden principperne fra DevOps og software engineering med i vores dataprojekter, hvilket betyder, at tests, Infrastructure as Code og automatisering er i fokus.
Hvilken rolle får du?
Du kommer til at arbejde på et af vores dataprojekter med et projektteam bestående af arkitekter, software udviklere og DevOps specialister. Du vil have fuldt fokus på ét projekt og være tilknyttet ét team ad gangen.
Din rolle bliver at
rådgive om og bygge datawarehouse/dataplatformsløsninger i skyenbidrage til design og implementering af den optimale Azure infrastruktursikre, at alt opsætning og provisionering sker som infrastructure as code, fx. Terraformfuldautomatiseret overvågning og skalering af løsninger
Hvad forventer vi af dig?
Du har en videregående uddannelse i software engineering, human centered AI el lign. fra DTU, ITU eller KU samt erfaring i data engineeringDu har fx. erfaring i at bygge datawarehouses og dataplatforme og har lyst til at arbejde med data engineeringDu har erfaring med - og vil gerne blive klogere på - flere af følgende teknologier: Data Factory, Databricks, Fabric, Spark, Azure Synapse, Data Warehouse, Data platform, Python, Azure DevOps, Powershell, Azure CLI, ARM templates, YAML, Bicep, Terraform, Git og CI/CDDu vil gerne arbejde med de nyeste teknologier i AzureDu vil gerne indgå i et agilt setup, hvor krav og retning hurtigt kan ændres, men hvor medbestemmelse og frihed er i højsædetDu er flydende i skrift og tale på dansk og engelsk, og trives med at have den daglige kontakt med kundenDu har evnen til at omsætte kundebehov til handling
Det vil være en fordel, hvis:Du er vant til at arbejde som konsulent i et udviklingsteam i tæt samarbejde med arkitekter og software udviklere
Som person er du selvstændig, ansvarsbevidst, initiativrig, eksekverende og serviceminded. Du er drevet af at lære nyt og vild med at holde dig ajour med de nyeste metoder og teknologier. Du er engageret og motiveret i alt hvad du foretager dig. Hvis du også er glad for at sparre med dygtige kollegaer og trives med at løfte i flok, vil du passe perfekt ind hos os.
Hvad tilbyder vi?
Du kommer til at indgå i vores voksende team, hvor høj faglighed, innovation og arbejdsglæde er i højsæde. Det høje faglige niveau blandt dine kollegaer giver en unik mulighed for at arbejde med de bedste, og vi tager din faglige udvikling alvorligt. Vi har en flad organisation med et minimum af administration, hvor ledelsens døre altid er åbne, og der er god plads til både bordfodbold, fredagsbar, frokost på kajen og et aktivt liv udenfor arbejdstid. Flere gange om året afholder vi hackathons, hvor vi i teams dykker ned i præcis de emner, som udviklerne finder interessante. En del af vores kultur er også, at vi gerne ser alle komme med input til hvordan vi kan optimere, udvikle og forbedre cVation.
Hvorfor vælge os som din nye arbejdsplads?
Vores fundament er løsninger bygget på Microsoft Azure Cloud. Alle vores medarbejdere mestrer cloud-native udvikling og DevOps. Vi arbejder i projekter og engagerer os hands-on i fælles teams med vores kunder for at sikre kvalitet og effektiv videndeling.
Vi er passionerede omkring at hjælpe vores kunder med at få det maksimale ud af cloudteknologien. For os er cloud ikke blot infrastruktur eller et nyt datacenter i skyen. Teknologien giver virksomheder mulighed for at skabe væsentligt mere værdi og arbejde med agilitet og innovation på en helt ny måde.
Vi har været med fra starten, da cloud blev udviklet og tilgængeligt. Vi tilbyder således vores årelange erfaring og dybe cloud ekspertise samt en værktøjskasse med alle vores best practices i form af accelerationsplatformen CADD. Den dækker over gennemprøvede værktøjer, rammeværk, effektive udviklingsmetoder og færdige automatiseringskomponenter. Alt dette for at sikre, at projekter kommer hurtigt og sikkert fra start.
Du vil blive en del af en virksomhed, som tror på, at alle har betydning, og at det skal være sjovt at gå på arbejde hver dag. Du vil få kollegaer, der er interesseret i at lære og har ønske om at blive de bedste til det, de gør. Vi tilbyder derfor gode muligheder for at udvikle dig.
Interesseret?
Klik på Easy Apply knappen, hvis du vil arbejde med de nyeste teknologier. Hvis du har under 5 års erfaring, bedes du også vedhæfte dine karakterblade i samme fil som dit CV. Vi behandler din ansøgning fortroligt og arrangerer løbende interviews.
Har du spørgsmål til jobbet, eller vil du høre mere om cVation, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte:
Maiken Pelby, Head of People & Culture, T/ 42612828, map@cvation.com
Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!

Title: Data & Systems Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Glamox is in a new era; Glamox 2.0. As a part of our Commercial Excellence program, we are ready for growth, and we are modernizing our ways-of-working.




We are looking for a technically strong, data-centric professional to join our HR team and take ownership of our HR data, reporting, complex process design, and HR systems. Even though you will be a part of the HR organization, this is not an HR role.




What is it then?

This is a unique opportunity for someone with a background in data analysis, software development, or similar to apply their technical expertise in a strategic HR context. You will be a ‘builder’, who collects requirements, and then codes/develops those requirements into models, automation flows or processes for the Global HR organization.

You will play a key role in shaping how we use data to drive decision-making across compensation, benefits, and broader HR processes. We are not looking for an HR profile for this role, but curiosity and interest in people-related topics will be an advantage—we will provide the necessary HR domain training.

Due to our M&A history and future growth strategy, Glamox is a medley of different systems, processes and data sets. The opportunity here is huge and your level of impact and autonomy will be high.




Key Responsibilities

·       Own and manage HR data, ensuring accuracy, structure, and accessibility

·       Design, build, and maintain reports, dashboards, and analytics

·       Lead compensation and benefits processes (e.g., annual salary reviews, bonus modelling)

·       Improve and optimize HR systems, workflows, and data architecture

·       Develop scalable data models to support HR decision-making

·       Partner with HR and leadership to translate business needs into data solutions

·       Identify opportunities for automation and process improvements. If we automate it, let’s do it.




Who You Are

·       Minimum 1-3 years of relevant work experience within data analysis, software development, or similar. A technical background is key for this role.

·       Advanced skills in data modeling, coding, and building solutions (e.g., Python, SQL, or similar)

·       Comfortable working with complex datasets and translating them into actionable insights

·       Experience with vibe coding, and using AI to generate solid solutions, is an big advantage.

·       Structured, analytical, and solution-oriented mindset

·       Interest in applying technology and structured solutions to people, organizational topics and the employee life cycle.

·       Effective communication skills and fluent in English.




For this role, you should be close to one of our main offices in Oslo, Stockholm, Copenhagen or Gothenburg. We will only accept applications from candidates who have valid work authorization in Norway, Denmark, or Sweden. This role reports to the Group CHRO.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
📍 Location: Baltorpvej (~20 mins from central Copenhagen)

🗣️ Language Requirement: English-speaking




Coders Connect is thrilled to be partnering with a global leader in smart pest control solutions, helping expand their Innovation Centre just outside Copenhagen.

You’ll join a startup-style unit within the company, focused on delivering next-gen IoT-powered, data-driven services.




With over 1 million IoT devices deployed globally, this team plays a critical role in transforming massive data volumes into actionable insights. We're seeking a Danish-speaking Data Engineer ready to work in a fast-paced, agile environment and build something that truly scales 🌍.




🔧 What You’ll Do

Build and maintain data pipelines that handle hourly batch data from a vast IoT device network
Work in Azure and Databricks to create scalable, high-performance cloud data infrastructure
Collaborate closely with Data Scientists, QA, Product Managers & Engineers in agile 2-week sprints
Contribute to architecture, ensuring top-notch data quality, performance, and reliability
Help transition from external consultants to an internal, capability-driven data team
Operate autonomously in a flat, startup-like structure




👨 💻 You Bring

Fluency in English (spoken & written)
Living in Denmark (No Visa support, unfortunately)
5+ Years in Data Engineering required with a slope to become Technical Lead
Proven experience with Azure Data Services and Databricks
Strong ETL pipeline skills for batch data processing and working with large data pipelines
A scale-up/startup mindset ~ proactive, self-driven, and ownership-focused
Experience in agile development environments




📍 Working Setup

Hybrid: Tuesdays onsite required, ideally 2–3 days/week
Office in Baltorpvej (approx. 20 mins from central Copenhagen)
Flat structure, high autonomy, quick decision-making




💸 What’s On Offer

Competitive Salary: DKK 65,000 - 75,000 + 10% pension
+10% employer pension contribution
5 weeks vacation + 1 extra week
Work on a meaningful, global-impact IoT product
High flexibility, real ownership, and influence from day one




❌ Please note: The company does not offer VISA sponsorship/transfer




If you're a Data Engineer looking for real-world impact at scale, in a team where your voice matters we’d love to hear from you!

  • Experience in agile development environments


📍 Working Setup

  • Hybrid: Tuesdays onsite required, ideally 2–3 days/week
  • Office in Baltorpvej (approx. 20 mins from central Copenhagen)
  • Flat structure, high autonomy, quick decision-making


💸 What’s On Offer

  • Competitive Salary: DKK 65,000 - 75,000 + 10% pension
  • +10% employer pension contribution
  • 5 weeks vacation + 1 extra week
  • Work on a meaningful, global-impact IoT product
  • High flexibility, real ownership, and influence from day one


❌ Please note: The company does not offer VISA sponsorship/transfer


If you're a Data Engineer looking for real-world impact at scale, in a team where your voice matters we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Annalect, we help businesses across industries make better decisions — from marketing strategies to business development and sales forecasting. Our approach combines deep industry understanding with advanced, AI-powered analytical methods, enabling us to deliver insights and recommendations that make a real difference for our clients. In Denmark, we are more than 70 specialists based in Copenhagen and Aarhus. As part of Omnicom Media Group, we have over 300 colleagues in Denmark and a global network of more than 5,000 experts with whom we collaborate to create the best possible solutions for our clients.

Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team

Are you passionate about applying Data Science in real-world scenarios? Do you want to leverage mathematics, statistics, and programming to help our clients make better decisions? Are you driven by data and analysis, and eager to translate insights into actionable recommendations? Join Annalect’s Data Science Consultancy team!

About The Role

We are looking for a new colleague to join Annalect, Omnicom Media Group’s specialist unit for data-driven analytics and decision support systems. Our work includes analyses that quantify the impact of our clients’ marketing activities and provide the foundation for ongoing optimization. Annalect consists of 60 employees, with 20 dedicated to the Data Science Consultancy team. Our team utilizes mathematics, statistics, and econometrics to process large volumes of data, delivering business-oriented decision support. As a Data Science Consultant, you will be involved in the entire project lifecycle for our clients – from data collection and coding, to building and optimizing statistical models in R, and communicating results and recommendations to clients. You will work closely with a project lead and the team, receiving ongoing support and feedback. As you gain experience, you will have the opportunity to conduct analyses independently, manage client communications, and deliver your own projects.

Key Responsibilities


Collect client data and extract data from media systems
Programming and data processing
Structure, code, and interpret data
Develop statistical models in R
Analyze results and prepare recommendations in close collaboration with a project lead
Prepare deliverables for clients in PowerPoint and dashboards
Maintain ongoing dialogue with clients and media agencies throughout the project
Lead your own projects


Your Profile

We Imagine That You


Hold a relevant academic degree (e.g., Cand.Merc. (MAT), Cand.polit. IMM, AEF, or similar)
Have basic experience with statistical models and methodologies
Possess strong analytical skills
Are proficient in R Studio (or other programming languages such as Python, SAS, VBA, SQL and are eager to further develop your skills in R Studio)
Are experienced in Excel and PowerPoint
Are interested in building an understanding of marketing, digital platforms, and the media landscape


What We Offer

We offer a position in an exciting company with many opportunities. You will be part of a strong professional community and work alongside some of the industry’s most talented specialists. We also have a vibrant social environment, including Friday bars, annual parties, company football team, DHL race, and more. Our culture is relaxed, with room for fun and focus for deep work on projects.

Interested?

We look forward to receiving your application and CV as soon as possible. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting once the right candidate has been found.

If you have any questions about the position or the company, please contact Analytics Director Anders Mousten at anders.mousten@omc.com.

Recruitment Privacy Notices - Omnicom Media
