Title: Student Analyst (SE Market) with Data Analysis Expertise
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join our Swedish Business Development team and put your analytical skills to work where they truly matter.

As a Student Analyst, you’ll work hands-on with data that supports real commercial decisions—helping identify product and market opportunities by transforming complex datasets into clear, actionable insights. If you’re studying economics, math, engineering, or a similar field and eager to strengthen your data toolkit, we’d love to hear from you.




Drive meaningful change

As a Student Analyst in the Swedish Business Development team, you will play an essential role in driving growth, developing operational procedures, and analyzing new potential products and market opportunities to grow our portfolios.

In this role, your focus is to strengthen our decision-making through structured analysis. You’ll work with data from multiple sources, build clear overviews, and translate findings into insights that help us prioritize the right products and market opportunities for Sweden.

You will experience a high degree of trust and responsibility in your job, making the work both challenging and rewarding.




Get a glimpse of our world

You will be part of a small, dynamic, and ambitious team focused on professional development, individual responsibility, team spirit, and an informal tone. Our team currently consists of three student analysts and six full-time employees.

We value diversity and believe that having varied perspectives drives innovation and growth. That’s why we’re looking for two distinct profiles for these roles to take our team’s impact to the next level.




Are you ready to embark on this journey with us?

You enjoy working in a commercial environment and want to support portfolio and product decisions through structured analysis. You are curious about markets and products—and you see data as a practical tool to help teams make better, more informed decisions rather than as an end in itself.

Are in the second or third year of your bachelor’s degree (e.g. economics, math, engineering, statistics, or a similar field)
Have strong analytical skills and a genuine interest in developing your data capabilities professionally
Maintain solid Microsoft Excel skills; experience with tools such as Power BI, SQL, or Python is a plus but not required
Enjoy working with larger datasets and creating clear, structured overviews
Work in a systematic way and care about accuracy, logic, and reproducibility
Can explain analyses and conclusions clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Speak a Nordic language; Swedish is preferred but not mandatory




Are you interested?

If this position sounds like a good match for you, we encourage you to submit your application.

We review applications on an ongoing basis and screening conversations may take place before the deadline. We therefore encourage you to apply as soon as possible. Interviews are expected to take place during April.




Would you like to know more about us?

Follow us on Instagram and LinkedIn and get an insight into everyday life at Abacus Medicine. For job openings, check our Career page.




Moving Healthcare. Moving You.

In Abacus Medicine Group, you’ll experience the power of Moving Healthcare. With our change mindset, we're moving the industry and improving healthcare as we broaden access to medicine.

But it's not just about moving healthcare, it's about Moving You. You’ll be part of a caring community of more than 1,500 people across the World. With us, you will be valued, empowered, and have a direct impact on driving change. Let's access the future of medicine and make a difference together. Your move.

Title: Student Worker, Discovery Data Science
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Genmab, we are dedicated to building extra[not]ordinary® futures, together, by developing antibody products and groundbreaking, knock-your-socks-off KYSO antibody medicines® that change lives and the future of cancer treatment and serious diseases. We strive to create, champion and maintain a global workplace where individuals’ unique contributions are valued and drive innovative solutions to meet the needs of our patients, care partners, families and employees.

Our people are compassionate, candid, and purposeful, and our business is innovative and rooted in science. We believe that being proudly authentic and determined to be our best is essential to fulfilling our purpose. Yes, our work is incredibly serious and impactful, but we have big ambitions, bring a ton of care to pursuing them, and have a lot of fun while doing so.

Does this inspire you and feel like a fit? Then we would love to have you join us!

The Role

Are you excited about the intersection of AI and cancer immunology, and looking to apply your technical skills in a real-world biotech setting over a longer period of time?

We are looking for a student worker (approx. 15 hours per week) to join our ML & AI for Antibody Discovery and Development team within the Discovery Data Science department in Copenhagen. This is a long-term position (minimum 18 months), designed to run alongside your studies and allow you to build depth, ownership, and impact over time.

In this role, you will be part of a multidisciplinary, international team of scientists with deep expertise in antibody development and machine learning. Together, you will contribute to the development of next-generation antibody therapeutics by combining experimental data with advanced computational approaches. You will work at the interface of data science, machine learning, and biology, collaborating closely with both dry- and wet-lab researchers. Over time, you will help shape and structure your own work, with the opportunity to take ownership of projects and contribute to scalable solutions within the team.

Does this sound like something that interests you? We look forward to your application! Help us get to know you by submitting a CV and motivational letter.

Responsibilities


Conduct exploratory data analysis and statistical analyses on biological datasets
Build, train, and evaluate machine learning models for antibody design and discovery
Experiment with methods, parameters, and datasets to improve model performance
Contribute to the development of scalable and reusable code within shared codebases
Translate complex technical results into clear and intuitive visualizations and presentations
Collaborate with scientists across disciplines to define questions, interpret results, and iterate on approaches
Over time, take ownership of (parts of) projects and help shape the structure of ongoing work within the team


Requirements


Currently enrolled in a BSc or MSc program in Computer Science, Engineering, Machine Learning, Data Science, or a related field
Based in Denmark and available for approximately 15 hours per week for a minimum of 18 months
Interest in or exposure to biological data (e.g. proteins, antibodies)
Strong programming skills in Python and experience working in a command-line environment
Solid understanding of statistics and machine learning principles
Experience with version control systems (e.g. Git)
Able to work independently and proactively, with a structured approach to problem-solving
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate in a multidisciplinary, international environment
Fluent in English (written and spoken)
Experience with cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, Databricks) - nice to have
Familiarity with MLOps tools (e.g. MLflow, Weights & Biases) - nice to have
Experience with structural biology or molecular modelling tools (e.g. PyMOL, Rosetta) - nice to have


Intended Recruitment Timeline

 


Application deadline: 13 April 2026
Shortlisting: week of 13 April 2026
Initial online screenings: week of 20 April 2026
Onsite interviews: week of 27 April 2026
Intended start date : 15 May/1 June


About You


You are genuinely passionate about our purpose
You bring precision and excellence to all that you do
You believe in our rooted-in-science approach to problem-solving
You are a generous collaborator who can work in teams with a broad spectrum of backgrounds
You take pride in enabling the best work of others on the team
You can grapple with the unknown and be innovative
You have experience working in a fast-growing, dynamic company (or a strong desire to)
You work hard and are not afraid to have a little fun while you do so!


Locations

Genmab maximizes the efficiency of an agile working environment, when possible, for the betterment of employee work-life balance. Our offices are crafted as open, community-based spaces that work to connect employees while being immersed in our powerful laboratories. Whether you’re in one of our office spaces or working remotely, we thrive on connecting with each other to innovate.

About Genmab

Genmab is an international biotechnology company with a core purpose to improve the lives of patients through innovative and differentiated antibody therapeutics. For 25 years, its hard-working, innovative and collaborative team has invented next-generation antibody technology platforms and harnessed translational, quantitative and data sciences, resulting in a proprietary pipeline including bispecific T-cell engagers, antibody-drug conjugates, next-generation immune checkpoint modulators and effector function-enhanced antibodies. By 2030, Genmab’s vision is to transform the lives of people with cancer and other serious diseases with Knock-Your-Socks-Off (KYSO®) antibody medicines.

Established in 1999, Genmab is headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark with international presence across North America, Europe and Asia Pacific. For more information, please visit Genmab.com and follow us on LinkedIn and X.

Genmab is committed to protecting your personal data and privacy. Please see our privacy policy for handling your data in connection with your application on our website Job Applicant Privacy Notice (genmab.com).

Title: Data Steward, Student Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Our purpose is to bring hope to life by enabling life-changing therapies for patients around the globe, creating a healthier and happier tomorrow. Our mission is to work side by side with our customers in order to improve patients’ lives by bringing new biopharmaceuticals to market.

As Data Steward in Global BD, you will be responsible for ensuring the quality, consistency, and appropriate availability of data within an organization.

You will typically work closely with business users like, sales team, operations team, analyst, and digitalization team to maintain trusted, usable, and well-documented data.

Key Responsibilities


Data Quality Improvement
Clean, validate, improve, and maintain CRM (Salesforce) data to have high accuracy, completeness, and consistency.
Monitor and report on data quality issues and propose solutions.
Salesforce Support (Ad-hoc)
Assist with ad-hoc data-related tasks such as list preparation, data extraction, or data feeds and import/export activities.
Support end-users with data entry best practices and basic troubleshooting.
Guiding users for right salesforce usage.
Digitalization & Process Optimization Support
Assist in documenting processes and workflows during digital transformation projects.
Help evaluate and test new tools or enhancements to Salesforce and other integration platforms.
Ad-hoc project support like quick summary reports.
Dashboards & Reporting (optional based on skillset)
Support the maintenance of dashboards and basic analytics using Salesforce reports or tools like Excel/Power BI.

Qualifications


Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or master’s program (preferably in Business, IT, Data Science, Engineering, or similar).
Strong interest in digitalization, CRM systems, and data-driven decision-making.
Experience or coursework in Excel, data analysis, or CRM tools (Salesforce or Power BI is a plus).
Attention to detail and a structured approach to tasks is a must.
Proactive attitude and willingness to learn is bonus.
Excellent communication skills in English (written and verbal).
Ability to handle confidential information with discretion.


Our culture at AGC Biologics is defined by the six core values: Knowledge, Trust, Quality, Ingenuity, Accountability and Teamwork. Our core values stem from our team members and are embedded into our DNA. They provide a common language and understanding of how we as an organization are connected across three continents. Our core values serve as a compass and reminder of how we achieve our purpose of bringing hope to life for patients around the globe.

AGC Biologics is a leading global biopharmaceutical Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) with a strong commitment to delivering the highest standard of service as we work side-by-side with our clients and partners, every step of the way. We provide world-class development and manufacture of mammalian and microbial-based therapeutic proteins, plasmid DNA (pDNA), messenger RNA (mRNA), viral vectors, and genetically engineered cells. Our global network spans the U.S., Europe, and Asia, with cGMP-compliant facilities in Seattle, Washington; Boulder and Longmont, Colorado; Copenhagen, Denmark; Heidelberg, Germany; Milan, Italy; and Chiba, Japan. We currently employ more than 2,500 employees worldwide. Our commitment to continuous innovation fosters the technical creativity to solve our clients’ most complex challenges, including specialization in fast-track projects and rare diseases. AGC Biologics is the partner of choice. To learn more, visit www.agcbio.com.  Want to keep posted about our growth and learn more about our company? Follow us on LinkedIn and give us a quick Like on Facebook!

AGC Biologics offers a highly competitive compensation package and a friendly, collaborative culture that values personal initiative and professional achievement. AGC Biologics is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.

Title: Data Analyst, Sourcing – Viby J
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Driving Digital Transformation Through Insightful Analytics

Are you passionate about bridging the gap between business stakeholders and our developers? As a Business Analyst at STARK GROUP IT, you will play a key role in enabling our Sourcing Analytics team in our digital transformation by analysing data and translating business requirements into tangible work for our developers. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Europe and on many different levels from Process Owners to Architects, ensuring our digitalisation stays on track. If you are ready to make a real impact and help STARK become a truly data-driven organization, we would love to hear from you.

Your Role and Impact

You will work closely with architects, data engineers, BI developers, and sourcing leaders within STARK Group IT and across our seven European business units. Your job is to translate business needs into clear technical specifications, ensure the right data teams are engaged, and guide initiatives from requirement to delivery. You will also support our Athene sourcing projects by analysing and documenting data sources and ensuring they are made available and tested.

In this role you will:


Collaborate with business and technical teams to uncover, challenge, and clarify data requirements while ensuring accurate documentation.
Translate business needs into structured, actionable technical specifications for development and data teams.
Identify and assess relevant data sources in cooperation with stakeholders and data domains and ensuring alignment with enterprise data management practices.
Ensure final solutions meet agreed objectives, manage change requests, and maintain progress across teams.
Facilitate alignment on KPIs and definitions, and ensure they are properly documented in the enterprise data catalog in line with Data Management CoE standards.


You will be successful in this role when you consistently ensure timely delivery and receive strong stakeholder feedback on both the outcome and the collaboration. By acting as a clear bridge between business and developers, you translate business needs into well-defined data requirements and actionable data analysis that create real business value. Your success is driven by strong communication, analytical thinking, and your ability to navigate a collaborative environment.

What You Bring

We are looking for someone who is structured, curious, and confident operating across both business and technical environments. You enjoy turning complexity into clarity and fostering alignment across teams.

You bring:


Strong ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders across organisational layers.
Proven experience translating business needs into technical data requirements and guiding datafocused projects.
Ability to evaluate and structure change requests, paired with strong attention to detail and a strategic mindset.
Familiarity with building templates or defining new ways of working, and basic skills in SQL and Databricks.
Experience working in international environments and navigating different cultures.


You will use a modern data stack that includes Azure Databricks, Power BI, Azure DevOps, Qlik Replicate, Kafka and our enterprise data catalog in Open Metadata. It is also helpful - but not required - if you have some familiarity with BI or Data Management processes, sourcing related domains, agile ways of working, ServiceNow, or Azure components such as Synapse or Data Factory.

Welcome to Group IT

When you join Group IT, you join a collaborative international organisation where technology and business go hand in hand. We are replacing legacy systems and building a modern, modular IT landscape to support STARK Group’s continued growth. You will become part of a community that values knowledge sharing, professionalism, and building solutions that make a clear difference to colleagues across Europe.

Want to know more and apply?

We are happy to answer any questions you may have regarding the role or your opportunities at STARK Group. Please reach out to Kristine Grann at Kristine.grann@stark.dk for more information.

STARK Group 

STARK Group is a growing private equity-owned company with a strong legacy. STARK Group was first established in Denmark in 1896 and has today evolved into a leading European retailer and distributor of heavy building materials with net sales of approx. EUR 9 billion. With a dedicated team of more than 20,000 employees and +1,150 branches and distribution centres across the Nordics, Greenland, Germany, Austria and the UK, we live and breathe to serve professional craftsmen with heavy building materials all the way from the Austrian Alps to Tromsø, close to the Arctic circle and everywhere in between. STARK Group is committed to reach net zero emissions by 2050 across the entire business, is a signatory to the UN Global Compact, and works within fully 2030 validated science-based targets to keep global warming below 1.5°C 

At STARK Group IT, we believe in the richness diversity brings. We invite individuals of all backgrounds to join us in creating an inclusive and welcoming environment. We believe in a workplace where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work. 

Agency Policy 

STARK Group IT appreciates the interest of all candidates. However, we prefer to directly engage with individuals seeking employment opportunities. Therefore, we kindly request recruitment agencies, headhunters, and third-party staffing services to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by the candidates through our official application process. We value direct communication and look forward to reviewing your application.

Title: Intern for Model Development
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you curious to learn how models are developed and implemented in the real world? Would you like to be the one to futureproof our processes by applying AI solutions? Are you keen to learn something new, prepared to be surprised and willing to contribute?

About Us

In Group Risk Analytics, we are building the next generation of credit risk models to help the bank and our customers better manage money in times of need. Our team consists of nine people from various countries, co-located in Copenhagen and Vilnius. With diverse backgrounds, we share a common drive to turn data into models that make sense of the world around us. Most of our time is spent coding in Python and, to a lesser extent, SQL, but we maintain close collaboration with stakeholders in finance, impairments, and credit risk management. The work is demanding but also meaningful, with a fun atmosphere—and the opportunity to drive concrete improvements.

About The Role

You will join a dynamic team where your skills or interest in data science, Python, and AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and CoPilot will be highly valued. You will actively participate in our meetings, contributing to statistical and coding discussions. Beyond this, you will connect with other teams to understand how we collaborate and suggest ways to enhance our projects. This is a fulltime internship of ideally 6 months. We imagine you are a student who can join as part of your studies as an academic intern.

Your key responsibilities:


Participate in team meetings to discuss statistical and coding matters.
Engage with other teams to understand cross-functional collaboration.
Suggest improvements to ongoing projects to optimise processes.
Support the development and implementation of AI and data-driven solutions.


About You

We are looking for someone who is structured, analytical, and ready to face challenges with curiosity and creativity. You should have a combination of technical skills, a collaborative mindset, and good communication abilities to thrive in this role.


Structured and analytical with good problem-solving skills
Able to manage their own workload with great collaboration skills and a proactive attitude
Understanding of data science and familiarity with Python
Knowledge of mainstream AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and CoPilot
Proficiency in verbal and written English, as our company language is English


We are hoping to hear from candidates who might have skills in areas such as entrepreneurial business engineering, business analytics or data science. You may possess skills in identifying and solving complex business challenges, applying advanced methods like optimisation and machine learning, or transforming data into actionable insights. You might also bring knowledge in developing innovative solutions, bridging data science with business strategy, or managing frameworks for digital business. Additionally, skills in implementing and optimising AI solutions to create value for organisations would be highly relevant.

Stephan Kim, Head of Super AI, says, "Join a dynamic team where curiosity, creativity, and collaboration are key. You’ll contribute to futureproofing processes with AI and data-driven solutions, driven by a collective drive to improve outcomes. Whether you bring experience in data science, Python, or AI tools—or simply a desire to learn—this role offers the chance to grow and deliver measurable results”.

Ready to join us?

Apply now! We process applications continuously, so we encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. If you have any questions about the job, please contact cc, Stephan Kim Jepsen at stje@danskebank.dk.

We look forward to your application and hopefully welcoming you to our team!

About Us

Danske Bank is a Nordic bank with bridges to the world around us. For 150 years, we have supported people and businesses in releasing their potential. A career with us is an opportunity to join a community of 22,000 colleagues in a culture where we are committed to Teaming Up, Owning It and Being Open. Together, we are on a journey to transform Danske Bank into a better bank. For our customers, our employees and the societies around us.

Title: Senior Data Specialist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you ready to help improve banking by building a solid, trusted data foundation that enables better customer experiences and smarter decisions across the bank? Join the Data Cluster, where data, technology, and collaboration come together to drive progress across the organization.

Join our team at the Customer Engagement Individuals Tribe:

The Personal Customers business unit in Danske Bank is undergoing a major transformation, heavily reliant on a sustainable, scalable, and secure data foundation. This foundation is a key enabler for reaching the bank’s Forward’28 strategic objectives, ensuring better banking experiences and more reliable products for our customers.

You will join the Customer Engagement Individuals (CEI) Tribe, where the customer is at the centre of everything we do. The tribe owns key customer-facing systems across Danske Bank’s channels and capabilities. We are a diverse group of approximately 160 colleagues spanning roles such as Data Analysts, Data Engineers, Business & Functional Specialists, Architects, UX Designers and more, working across the Nordics, Lithuania, and India.

Who are we looking for?

We imagine you are a collaborative and analytical professional who enjoys working in complex environments, bridging business needs and technical solutions, and creating clarity where others see ambiguity.

About the Role:

Danske Bank is seeking a Business & Functional Specialist to join one of our Data Foundation squads within the Customer Engagement Individuals Tribe. In this role, you will play a central part in enabling high-quality, trusted data across the Personal Customers domain, forming the backbone for next-generation banking services.

You will work at the intersection of business, data, and technology, translating complex business needs into clear and sustainable data specifications. By doing so, you will help commercial teams unlock value from data and ensure system owners can rely on consistent, compliant, and high-quality data for downstream use cases.

A normal workday for our business functional specialist will consist of working in cross collaboration with your team of data experts and business & system stakeholders ensuring the business requirements are translated properly into technical specifications to ensure sustainable delivery of data are across the bank. This will enable us to deliver on high quality customer products as well as enable us to become more digital as outlines in the Forward ’28 strategy. Also, it will consist of engaging with a wide range of stakeholders to serve both upstream and downstream business needs through data.

Danske Bank supports a high degree of workplace flexibility (our team is currently using a hybrid working model, where we work at least 3 days a week in the office.). Location is Copenhagen, Århus, Vilnius or Helsinki. No relocation is offered.

You will:


Work closely with business stakeholders, system owners, and data owners to ensure prioritised activities that support better customer experiences
Translate business logic and functional requirements into clear data specifications that support sustainable golden-source data models
Collaborate across the bank with both technical and non-technical stakeholders to help drive the bank-wide data agenda in line with the Forward’28 strategy
Enable downstream stakeholders to realise business value by ensuring timely and reliable data availability together with the team
Ensure thorough, coherent documentation and adherence to governance and compliance standards within the subject area
Contribute to the transformation of Danske Bank’s retail customer area to support the goals set out in Forward’28
Continuously learn and grow in the role, working to deliver a high-quality experience for customers
Help ensure alignment with regulatory, governance, and security frameworks, enabling end-to-end compliant, accessible, and secure data


About you:


Bachelor’s degree or higher within Business, Engineering, Natural/Life Sciences, Engineering or similar
Experience as a Business Analyst, Business & Functional Specialist, or in a similar role
Experience with security, compliance, risk, and financial services data is beneficial
Proficiencies in data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, or similar is beneficial
Analytical, able to manage shifting priorities, and comfortable working in uncertain and changing environments
A collaborative mindset with strong facilitation skills
For more senior profiles, strong organizational understanding and strategic thinking are key
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with fluency in spoken and written English


We offer:

Monthly salary range: this position can be located both in Lithuania, Finland and Denmark. If you are hired in Lithuania, the applicable salary range will be from 3920 to 5880 EUR monthly (based on your competencies relevant for the job).

Additionally, each Danske Bank employee receives employee benefits package which includes:


Growth opportunities: professional & supportive team, e-learnings, numerous development programs; (incl. professional certificates); 100+ professions for internal mobility opportunities.
Health & Well-being: a diverse, inclusive, work & life balance work environment; additional health insurance; mental well-being practices; partial psychologist counselling compensation; silence and sleep zones at the office; game rooms.
Hybrid working conditions: Work from home up to two days a week; home office budget (after the probation period); modern Danske Campus workplace developed with anthropologist for the best employee experience.
Additional days of leave: for rest, health, volunteering, exams in higher education institutions, and other important activities. Moreover, for seniority with Danske Bank.
Monetary compensation package: accidents & critical diseases insurance; financial support in case of unfortunate events, travel insurance; IIIrd Pillar Pension Fund contribution.


See all the benefits HERE.

Your job function (position) in the job contract will be Technology Business Solutions.

Have we caught your attention? Apply now!

We process applications continuously, so we encourage you to apply sooner rather than later.

For more information, please contact Hiring Managers Michael Friis Bak on mras@danskebank.dk or Interim Product Owner Freja Hallund Slot on frsl@danskebank.dk

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Familiarity and advocacy of AI tooling (ideally Claude)
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.


At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.


What you’ll do

  • Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
  • Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
  • Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
  • Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
  • Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
  • Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
  • Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
  • Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
  • As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.


What you have

  • Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
  • Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
  • Proven experience in product or operational analytics
  • Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
  • Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
  • Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
  • Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
  • Familiarity and advocacy of AI tooling (ideally Claude)
  • Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts


Nice to haves

  • Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
  • Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
  • Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
  • Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems


Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.


What we offer

  • The best team in the world!
  • An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
  • Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
  • A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
  • Private health insurance for you and your family members.
  • A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
  • Lunch compensation.
  • Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
  • Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
  • Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Data Collector
Place: Denmark
Description:
Global Freelance Opportunity | Gig Workers Needed for AI Data Collection




We are currently recruiting gig-workers across multiple countries to support a global AI training data project.




The task is simple: capture photos of everyday objects at home and upload them to our platform.




No professional photography experience is required.




💰 Compensation

Earn $0.28 per approved image

Typical contributors upload 100–500 images, allowing you to earn $30–$140 per session depending on participation.




📸 What You Will Do

Take clear photos of common objects around you
Upload them with the object name and image details(Dropdowns)
Follow simple submission guidelines




📦 Example Categories

We are collecting images across many real-world product categories, including:

Household Items, Electronics, Clothing, Furniture, Food & Beverage, Tools & Hardware, Toys, Books, Musical Instruments, Baby Products, Medical Items, Cleaning Products, Eyewear, Retail Objects, Signs, Watches, Containers, and more.




🌎 Project Scope

This is a large-scale international project, with each country contributing 4,000 – 16,000 images.

We are looking for hundreds of contributors globally.




👤 Ideal For

Freelancers
Students
Gig workers
Remote workers
Anyone looking for flexible side income




📩 How To Join

Step 1 - Apply by registering on our Gig-Sourcing Platform (Click Here)

Step 2 – Apply for the project “Simple Image Collection - Chester Global”

Step 3 - Communication & Support Channel - https://discord.gg/BkYahnm34

Step 4 – Download data collection mobile app – AIDAC (Available for iOS & Android)

Step 5 – Video for AIDAC usage - https://app.box.com/s/3d8xlbhw32qt5x6sy50wcbk39bgyw7ur

Step 6 – Use the same email ID for data submission on AIDAC

Step 7 – Get started 😊

**Spots are limited per country, so early registration is recommended.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job ID: 2785

Would you like to be a part of Nordea Asset Management’s journey towards modern data engineering era? We are looking for a data engineer to drive the ambitions of End- Of-Day Value stream who strives to provide the best quality data for various reporting requirements.

Nordea Asset Management (NAM) is the center of investment management in the Nordea Group and in the Asset & Wealth Management division. NAM is the largest asset manager in the Nordics with the vision of being a leading European Asset Manager. We want to do this by delivering returns with responsibility. We meet customer demands and guide our customers in the right direction in asset selection while ensuring prudent ESG practices. NAM has around 900 employees and more than 475 Nordic and international institutional clients.

About Our Team

Meet the Calculations and Risk Engineering team. Our role is to build data and reporting solutions using market’s best data engineering technologies.

Our team includes thirteen SW developers, proficient in skills and competences to support the associated business area domain with robust SW solutions.

As our new data engineer, you will get opportunity to collaborate with fellow engineers on market’s newest technological competences, engineering tools and processes. On day to day basis you will engage with End-Of-Day value stream, where competences from different areas work together to deliver data driven solutions. We are organized based on the concept of “you own it, you build it, you run it” and there is strong focus on an effective flow of value towards our consumers.

Our organization is currently going through a technological and data transformation and our engineers play a very important role in ensuring we grasp every possibility of transitioning towards the target in the everyday tasks to be solved.

What You’ll Be Doing

Some of your tasks at high level will be:


Design, develop and maintain data solutions/pipelines.
Understanding the domains data and supporting the modelling to be able to make data fit in the bigger picture.
Creating framework or reusable modules to enable business to create value faster.
Refactoring of current data pipelines and reporting solutions to the new platform/technologies.
Leveraging AI.
Participate and contribute to value streams ceremonies like product increment planning, status updates etc.
Be a proactive player in finding the right initiatives of technological transformation in the business domain.


Who You Are

Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.

We seek a data engineer, with a sound grasp of technologies and ability to bridge the gap between technical debt and modern solutions.

To succeed in this role, we believe that you:


Demonstrate high level of data engineering skills and believe in applying best engineering practices like automatic testing and continuous integration
Practice dev/data-ops as a natural choice for delivering outcomes
Are familiar with Data engineering principles, data pipelines and are acquainted to the concept of Data Mesh
Are curious to onboard on new technologies
Are able to find joy in a hybrid world of legacy and new solutions
Do not hesitate to take on new challenges


Your experience and background:


Fundamental knowledge of technologies like Airflow, dbt and dlt.
Hands on in coding language Python.
Have good understanding of modern integration patterns like REST APIs and Apache Kafka
Knowledge of AI/ML Concepts
Familiarity with developer tools like excel and SQL
Familiarity with Data warehouse and Data Lakehouse technologies; hands on experience with Snowflake is a bonus
Have a good understanding of cloud architecture
Have knowledge of CI-CD pipelines
Are fluent in English


What We Offer

People are driven by many different factors. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.

A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.

Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.

Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.

If this sounds like you, get in touch!

Next steps

Submit your application no later than 15/04/2026. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Head of Calculations and Risk Engineering Mandeep Kaur at mandeep.kaur@nordea.com.

Please be aware that any applications or CVs coming through email or direct messages will not be accepted or considered.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Place: Denmark
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $22 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

Title: Business/Data Analyst Praktikanter – Dataanalyse, Forecasting eller Backend
Place: Odense, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vi søger praktikanter – Dataanalyse, Forecasting eller Backend

Business/Data Analyst Praktikanter – Dataanalyse, Forecasting eller Backend

Har du passion for data og vil du arbejde med konkrete problemstillinger i en moderne dataplatform i forbindelse med din praktik? Vi søger nysgerrige og engagerede praktikanter, der vil være med til at skabe indsigt og værdi gennem analyse, forecasting og visualisering i bl.a. Power BI.

Om os

Du bliver en del af Business Insights, som er en enhed i afdelingen People & Analytics hos YouSee. Vi arbejder med dataanalyse, surveyindsigter og visualisering af KPI’er, der dækker mange af Nuudays forretningsområder. Vi understøtter ledelsen og forretningen med databaserede beslutninger og har fokus på både kvalitet og relevans i de indsigter, vi leverer.

Du får en praktikvejleder, som vil støtte dig gennem hele forløbet, herunder dit praktikprojekt, som vi tilpasser til dine interesser og uddannelsesbehov.

Dine opgaver

Som praktikant vil du kunne arbejde med én eller flere af følgende opgaver – afhængigt af din profil og interesser:


Analyse af kunde- og forretningsdata
Udvikling og vedligeholdelse af dashboards og rapporter
Understøttelse af surveyprojekter med fokus på kundeoplevelser (CX)
Forecasting og statistisk modellering
Backend-udvikling og stabilisering af dataløsninger i Azure og Databricks
Udtræk, rensning og strukturering af data
Deltagelse i ad hoc-analyser og præsentationer til stakeholders



Værktøjer du får mulighed for at arbejde med


SQL (inkl. T-SQL og stored procedures)
Python og AI (til dataanalyse, statistik, API-kald m.m.)
Power BI og DAX
Excel og surveyværktøjet Forsta (ConfirmIT)
Azure Data Factory, Databricks og relaterede cloud-løsninger



Vi forestiller os, at du


Er i gang med en relevant videregående uddannelse (f.eks. datalogi, business analytics, IT & økonomi) og skal i praktik
Har lyst til at bruge og udvikle dine kompetencer inden for dataanalyse og visualisering
Er nysgerrig, struktureret og motiveres af at lære nyt
Trives i et teamorienteret miljø og bidrager positivt til samarbejdet
Kan tage ansvar for egne opgaver og følge dem til dørs
Har kendskab til nogle af de nævnte værktøjer – det er ikke et krav, at du mestrer alle



Vi tilbyder


Et stærkt fagligt miljø med fokus på sparring, udvikling og læring
Et stærkt socialt kollegaskab både på og udenfor arbejde. Herunder flere planlagte arrangementer i afdelingen og i hele virksomheden.
Praktisk erfaring med moderne værktøjer som Azure, Databricks, Power BI, Python, AI og Forsta
Reelle opgaver med betydning for forretningen – ikke bare ”øvelser”
Mulighed for at skræddersy dine opgaver efter dine interesser og mål
Fleksibilitet ift. opstart og varighed – dog ser vi gerne minimum 4 måneders praktik
Praktikken er ulønnet



Din ansøgning

Send din ansøgning og CV snarest muligt – vi indkalder løbende til samtale. Praktikken foregår på dansk, så derfor skal ansøgningen også være på dansk.

Du må meget gerne nævne i din ansøgning, hvilke erfaringer og kompetencer du allerede har, samt hvad du brænder for at lære mere om. Det hjælper os med at tilpasse praktikken til dig.

Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig! Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte Anders Bjerrum på anden@yousee.dk.

Ansøgningsfrist: 31. maj 2026

Startdato: 1. august 2026 eller efter nærmere aftale

Arbejdssted: Sletvej 30, 8310 Tranbjerg J el. Munnkemølle Stræde 6, 5000 Odense

For yderligere information: Anders Bjerrum, mail: anden@yousee.dk

Nuuday – We make sense with technology

Hos Nuuday er vi 3.000 dedikerede medarbejdere, der på tværs af vores seks brands – YouSee, TDC Erhverv, Telmore, Hiper, eesy og Relatel – arbejder målrettet på at sætte retningen for Danmarks digitale fremtid. Vi befinder os midt i en spændende transformation, hvor vi ikke blot revolutionerer vores IT-systemer, men også skaber innovative digitale kundeoplevelser, der sætter nye standarder for, hvordan danskere, virksomheder og samfundet interagerer med teknologi i hverdagen. Hos Nuuday finder du en kultur præget af mangfoldighed, samarbejde og udvikling. Uanset hvor du arbejder, eller hvilket brand du er en del af, er én ting fælles for os alle: en passion for at skabe løsninger, der gør en forskel. Vi sætter en ære i at forstå vores kunder, og vi ser fremtiden som en mulighed for konstant at udvikle og forbedre os. Vi tror på, at et stærkt fællesskab lægger fundamentet til store resultater, og hos os er der altid plads til dem, der tør tænke nyt, tage ansvar og være med til at bane vejen. Vil du være med?

#YouSee

Nuuday – We make sense with technology

Hos Nuuday er vi 3.000 dedikerede medarbejdere, der på tværs af vores seks brands – YouSee, TDC Erhverv, Telmore, Hiper, eesy og Relatel – arbejder målrettet på at sætte retningen for Danmarks digitale fremtid. Vi befinder os midt i en spændende transformation, hvor vi ikke blot revolutionerer vores IT-systemer, men også skaber innovative digitale kundeoplevelser, der sætter nye standarder for, hvordan danskere, virksomheder og samfundet interagerer med teknologi i hverdagen. Hos Nuuday finder du en kultur præget af mangfoldighed, samarbejde og udvikling. Uanset hvor du arbejder, eller hvilket brand du er en del af, er én ting fælles for os alle: en passion for at skabe løsninger, der gør en forskel. Vi sætter en ære i at forstå vores kunder, og vi ser fremtiden som en mulighed for konstant at udvikle og forbedre os. Vi tror på, at et stærkt fællesskab lægger fundamentet til store resultater, og hos os er der altid plads til dem, der tør tænke nyt, tage ansvar og være med til at bane vejen. Vil du være med?

Title: Solution Manager, Data & Analytics
Place: Frederiksberg Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Fiskars Group we are unified and driven by our common purpose: pioneering design to make the everyday extraordinary. Join our team and seize the extraordinary opportunity to influence the everyday!

We’re looking for a Solution Manager, Data & Analytics to help shape the future of our IT systems. In this role, you will be working as part of team of Solution Managers to deliver robust, scalable solutions that support our business. At Fiskars, we value people with a growth mindset so you don’t need to know everything once you start; just bring your curiosity, drive, and a collaborative attitude and we will support your learning and growth.

Your Role


Act as a bridge between business and IT to define Data & Analytics solutions.
Lead projects to deliver end-to-end solutions
Support IT transformation by aligning system design with business needs.
Help define a future-proof Data & Analytics solution roadmap.
Participate in global projects and continuous improvement initiatives.
Maintain solution documentation and guide configuration efforts.
Assist in major incident handling and problem resolution.
Learn, grow, and contribute with a curious and collaborative mindset.



Key Responsibilities


Translate business needs into scalable Data & Analytics solutions.
Drive solution design aligned with best practices and enterprise architecture.
Guide internal/external teams on configuration and development.
Support change management, release cycles, and solution lifecycle.
Ensure solution usability, performance, and security.
Collaborate with architects, Business Process Managers (BPM), and partners to deliver high-quality results.
Identify new opportunities and propose improvements.
Contribute to Data & Analytics processes such as AutoML.



What You Bring


3+ years in Data & Analytics development and delivery.
Strong hands-on experience in solution configuration.
Understanding of global templates and end-to-end implementations.
Experience with tools like Fabric, Azure, Databricks, PowerBI and Jira
Knowledge of Data & Analytics integrations.
Strong communication and collaboration skills in English.
Ability to work in a global, cross-functional environment.
Passion for continuous learning and improving business through Data & Analytics.



Apply now!

Excited about this role? So are we! We are reviewing applications on a continuous basis so please submit your application already today!

Last day of application:

At Fiskars, we value providing benefits that matter to you and cater to our diverse global workforce, no matter where you are in the world. While benefits may vary based on location, here are some of the incredible offerings you can expect when joining our team:

Competitive Compensation: We ensure that our employees are fairly compensated based on industry standards and local market factors.

Health and Wellness: Your well-being matters to us, no matter where you are. We offer various health and well-being focused programs and coverage, adapted to the requirements of each region.

Work-Life Balance: We understand the need for balance in your life. Our paid time off plans and flexible work arrangements and policies are designed to accommodate local customs and personal commitments, enabling you to thrive in both your professional and personal life.

Professional Development: We believe in nurturing talent and supporting professional growth. Through training programs, skill development initiatives and learning resources, we empower our employees to reach their full potential.

Inclusive and Diverse Environment: We value diversity and promote inclusivity. Join a workplace where your unique background and perspectives are celebrated, fostering collaboration and respect across all our markets.

Recognition and Rewards: We believe in celebrating and recognizing the contributions of our employees worldwide. Our recognition programs are designed to acknowledge exceptional performance and achievements, ensuring that your efforts are celebrated and valued.

Vested Interest: Through our Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP), you have the opportunity to invest in Fiskars Group and become a proud co-owner, sharing in the success and growth of the company.

Employee Discounts: We want to ensure our team members make each day the extraordinary with use of our products and offer product discounts across our brands.

Please note that specific benefits may vary based on local regulations, market conditions, and other factors. We are committed to providing competitive and meaningful benefits to our employees in every market we operate.

Pioneering design to make the everyday extraordinary

Fiskars Group (FSKRS, Nasdaq Helsinki) is the global home of design-driven brands for indoor and outdoor living. Since 1649, we have designed products of timeless, purposeful, and functional beauty, while driving innovation and sustainable growth. In 2024, Fiskars Group’s global net sales were EUR 1.2 billion and we had close to 7,000 employees. We have two Business Areas (BA), Vita and Fiskars.

BA Vita offers premium and luxury products for the tableware, drinkware, jewelry and interior categories. Its well-known brands include Georg Jensen, Royal Copenhagen, Wedgwood, Moomin Arabia, Iittala and Waterford. In 2024, BA Vita’s reported net sales were EUR 605 million. Already 50% of BA Vita’s net sales comes from direct-to-consumer sales, comprising approximately 500 stores and approximately 60 e-commerce sites.

BA Fiskars consists of the gardening and outdoor categories, in addition to the scissors and creating, as well as cooking categories. The brands include Fiskars and Gerber. In 2024, BA Fiskars’ net sales were EUR 547 million.

Title: Quantitative Analyst
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Do you want to gain a deep understanding of the power market and contribute directly to trading decisions? Do you thrive in a high‑paced, data‑driven environment where your analytical skills shape tomorrow’s strategies? If you’re ready for a role with real responsibility and impact, join our team as a Quantitative Analyst and help drive disciplined, high‑quality trading outcomes.




We are seeking a highly motivated individual with strong quantitative skills to join our team as a Quantitative Analyst. In this role, you will work closely with the team, applying data‑driven insights, modelling, and risk thinking to take ownership of disciplined trading decisions. Your ability to transform analysis and code into actionable insights will directly contribute to improving the quality and profitability of our trading decisions.




In this role, you will gain deep insights into our trading strategies and tools that provide a significant competitive advantage. You can expect a steep learning curve and growth opportunities in an environment where flat hierarchy and team spirit drive our success. You will experience a high degree of autonomy and see your own ideas come to life and see your ideas quickly evolve into tangible solutions that make a meaningful impact on our trading operations.




Key Responsibilities

Market Analysis: Conduct in-depth, data‑driven analysis of power markets including supply and demand fundamentals, regulatory developments, weather‑driven patterns, and geopolitical impacts to uncover actionable trading opportunities and support strategic decision‑making.
Risk Management: Implement robust risk management practices by monitoring position limits, conducting stress testing, and assessing exposure to volatile market conditions to ensure controlled and compliant trading activities.
Tool Development: Develop, maintain, and optimize a range of analytical, real‑time monitoring, and reporting tools that enhance trading decision‑making and operational efficiency. Build automated dashboards and data pipelines to produce accurate, timely reports on trading performance, risk metrics, and key market trends.
Model Development: Continuously improve trading models through back testing and market‑driven data analysis to strengthen and support profitable trading decisions.
Collaboration: Work closely with the team to enhance trading strategies and technical setup, ensuring clear and consistent communication across all team members.
Trading Application: Apply insights from analysis, modelling and risk assessment to confidently support and execute well‑founded trading decisions that meet all required risk, compliance, and performance standards.




Qualifications and Requirements

As a candidate, we expect you:

Hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in mathematics‑economics, economics, statistics, engineering, or another quantitative field.
Have strong quantitative and analytical skills to interpret complex data and translate it into well‑founded trading insights.
Are proficient in Python and comfortable with coding. Experience with other code languages is considered an advantage.
Have a strong interest in financial and energy markets and a desire to deepen your understanding of power‑market dynamics.
Demonstrate excellent problem‑solving skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to adapt quickly to changing market conditions.
Communicate clearly and effectively, presenting technical and analytical concepts in a way that is easy for both technical and non‑technical team members to understand.




What We Offer

As an employer, we offer:

A full-time position that gives you the opportunity to apply your acquired skills.
Support in your role from experienced professionals.
Access to unique technologies and resources.
An opportunity to work at the forefront of power trading, contributing to groundbreaking strategies.
Opportunities for career growth and advancement.
Competitive compensation package.
A modern office in the heart of Aarhus.
Professional and committed colleagues with high ambitions.
Inspiring, collaborative, and innovative work environment.
A place where your contributions are recognized and have a meaningful impact on the company's success.




A Profitable Growth Company with a Clear Vision

ENZEE is an ambitious growth company specialized in energy trading across the European continent. We envision a sustainable world and therefore, every day at ENZEE is a day with purpose. We belief that well-functioning and efficient energy markets are a necessity towards a net zero future which is exactly where we contribute with our expertise.




The ENZEE team consists of highly qualified, experienced, and knowledgeable industry professionals with a wide range of expertise in the energy markets. As a member of our team, you can expect a steep learning curve, great career growth opportunities, and the possibility of making a significant contribution.




Jobstart & Type

As soon as possible. We will be screening candidates on an ongoing basis
Full-time position
Location: Aarhus




ENZEE is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace and therefore we encourage all candidates to apply, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, or disabilities.




For further inquiries, please contact Emmett Leach: +45 42 55 31 12

Title: Data Collector
Place: Denmark
Description:
Get paid to submit photos and screenshots of expired loyalty cards, event tickets, gift cards, and coupons with barcodes. Simple task, remote, global participation.




We are hiring freelance contributors worldwide to help us collect real photos and screenshots of expired cards, passes, and tickets with barcodes for a global research project.

This is a remote, flexible, task-based project where you submit images you already own or can easily capture.




📌 What you will submit

You will upload photos or screenshots of:

Membership / Loyalty cards (gym, library, store rewards, etc.)
Event tickets / passes (concerts, sports, exhibitions, conferences, etc.)
Gift cards (expired or used only)
Coupons (expired or already used)

**Every item must contain a visible barcode.

***Only real data — no AI-generated or internet images allowed.




📌 Basic Requirements

Must be 18+ years old
Must own the cards/tickets you submit
Cards/passes must be expired, used, or zero balance
Barcode must be clearly visible and readable
Images must be original and not downloaded from the internet




📌 Image Guidelines (Simple)

Take clear photos in good lighting
Show the full card/pass — no cropping
Different backgrounds encouraged (table, hand, wall, indoor/outdoor, etc.)
Screenshots must show merchant name + barcode
Do NOT use Apple Wallet screenshots
Accepted formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG, HEIC

📌 Do's:

Barcode should be visible and not obstructed by any element
Merchant name should be visible along with barcode, else capture other side where Merchant name is given
All Physical assets needs to be in jpg, jpeg, png and heic extensions only
Location on Phone should be switched on to capture EXIF Data (Latitude and Longitude)
Do not edit Photos post capturing which will remove details of exif
Physical assets are only Photo Images and not Screenshots or picture of picture
The orientation of the physical image should be straight and not curved or bended

📌 Dont's:

Photos captured should be strictly from Physical cards
No paper cards or printed cards are allowed
Strictly avoid Web scraping or fabrication of assets
Assets created using generated AI or derived from synthetic data will be rejected
Exif data is mandatory and any Photo with no exif data will be rejected




🌍 Target Countries

We are especially looking for contributors from:

China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, Turkey, Israel, Thailand, Malaysia, India, Brazil, Portugal, France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Netherlands, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and more.




💰 Payment

Paid per accepted asset
More valid submissions = higher total payout




📩 How To Apply

Step 1 - Apply by registering on our Gig-Sourcing Platform (Click Here)

Step 2 – Apply for the project “Vector Bar Codes Global – Mar 2026”

Step 3 - Communication & Support Channel - https://discord.gg/WrtbhUZMU

Step 4 – Download data collection mobile app – AIDAC (Available for iOS & Android)

Step 5 – Video for AIDAC usage - https://app.box.com/s/3d8xlbhw32qt5x6sy50wcbk39bgyw7ur

Step 6 – Use the same email ID for data submission on AIDAC

Step 7 – Get started 😊

**Spots are limited per country, so early registration is recommended.

Title: Student Assistant - Data analysis
Place: Fredericia, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Studentermedhjælper til dataanalyse

Er du nysgerrig på, hvordan markedsdata og forbrugerindsigt bliver til virkelighed bag kulisserne hos to af Danmarks førende kaffemærker, Merrild og Lavazza? Hos Lavazza Danmark får du muligheden for omsætte dine studiefærdigheder og dykke ned i analyser, tal og trends - i en spændende rolle, hvor du hvor du i tæt samarbejde med vores Senior Research Manager bidrager til at skabe værdifuld viden og indsigt. Lyder dette som noget for dig? Så vil vi meget gerne høre fra dig!




Om stillingen

Som studentermedhjælper hos os bliver du en del af vores marketingteam, som holder til i Fredericia. Din arbejdstid vil være 15 timer pr. uge, fordelt på 2 dage om ugen, og vil som udgangspunkt være på vores kontor. På sigt vil der være mulighed for nogle hjemmearbejdsdage.




Dine primære arbejdsopgaver vil være at:

Udtrække markedsdata og opdatere ugentlige og månedlige rapporter, primært i Microsoft Excel og PowerPoint: Heraf udvikling og optimering af vores standard rapport pakke.
Assistere ved og på sigt gennemføre dybdegående analyse af brands, kategorier, trends og markeder på tværs af analyseværktøjer og diverse rapporter.
Blive rutineret bruger af vores dataværktøjspakke Nielsen Discover, Kauza samt andre analyseværktøjer og med tiden håndtere forespørgsler fra marketing og salg.
Administrere og vedligeholde stamdata til diverse dataværktøjer.




Om dig

Vi søger en studerende, der er i gang med 2. eller 3. år af en relevant bacheloruddannelse, har interesse for tal og analyse, og som allerede nu har planer om at fortsætte på en kandidat i forlængelse af din bachelor.




Vi lægger herudover vægt på, at du:

Har en analytisk og datadrevet tilgang til dit arbejde.
Evner at tilgå dine opgaver med fordybelse og opmærksomhed på detaljer.
Trives med at planlægge og udføre egne opgaver.
Evner at skabe struktur i dine arbejdsopgaver og på din arbejdsfordeling.
Behersker engelsk i tale og skrift ubesværet.




Vi sikrer grundig introduktion og opgaveoverdragelse, så du kan føle dig godt klædt på til at varetage opgaverne.




Vi tilbyder

Hos Lavazza Denmark er vi engageret omkring det, vi laver. Som en organisation i udvikling, arbejder vi hver dag fokuseret på at udvikle forretningen og støtte hinanden. Vi tror på, at positive resultater skabes af mennesker, der trives – og vi prioriterer vi din personlige og faglige udvikling som en integreret del af vores strategi og hverdag.




Vi har fokus på en inkluderende kultur, hvor alle oplever, at vores værdier: Authenticity, Passion for Excellence, Responsibility og Inventiveness leves i dagligdagen.

Hos os får du mulighed for at være med til at udvikle en virksomhed, hvor engagement, kvalitet og fremdrift går hånd i hånd.




Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål til stillingen, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte Senior Research Manager, Birgitte Hein på +45 50 59 91 10.




Vi beder dig sende din ansøgning, CV samt dokumentation for igangværende uddannelse. Vi behandler ansøgningerne løbende, og besætter stillingen, når vi har den rette kandidat.




Om os

Lavazza Denmark er en del af Lavazza Group, der har produceret ægte italiensk kaffe i 130 år. Luigi Lavazza S.P.A. er blandt verdens førende kaffeleverandører, med en årlig omsætning på € 3.1 milliarder og mere end 5.500 dedikerede medarbejdere, der deler Lavazza passionen for at tilbyde forbrugerne den ypperste kaffekvalitet og kaffenydelse.




Lavazza Denmark er beliggende i Fredericia. Vi er 88 medarbejdere i Lavazza Denmark, som er hovedkontor for de nordiske organisationer.




Lavazza er en familiejet koncern, etableret i 1895, med hovedsæde i Torino i Italien. Lavazza er Italiens førende kaffemærke. Virksomhedens kaffeprodukter nydes endvidere i mere end 90 lande verden over. www.merrild.dk – www.lavazza.dk




#Fredericia

Show more
HTML:
Studentermedhjælper til dataanalyse

Er du nysgerrig på, hvordan markedsdata og forbrugerindsigt bliver til virkelighed bag kulisserne hos to af Danmarks førende kaffemærker, Merrild og Lavazza? Hos Lavazza Danmark får du muligheden for omsætte dine studiefærdigheder og dykke ned i analyser, tal og trends - i en spændende rolle, hvor du hvor du i tæt samarbejde med vores Senior Research Manager bidrager til at skabe værdifuld viden og indsigt. Lyder dette som noget for dig? Så vil vi meget gerne høre fra dig!


Om stillingen

Som studentermedhjælper hos os bliver du en del af vores marketingteam, som holder til i Fredericia. Din arbejdstid vil være 15 timer pr. uge, fordelt på 2 dage om ugen, og vil som udgangspunkt være på vores kontor. På sigt vil der være mulighed for nogle hjemmearbejdsdage.


Dine primære arbejdsopgaver vil være at:

  • Udtrække markedsdata og opdatere ugentlige og månedlige rapporter, primært i Microsoft Excel og PowerPoint: Heraf udvikling og optimering af vores standard rapport pakke.
  • Assistere ved og på sigt gennemføre dybdegående analyse af brands, kategorier, trends og markeder på tværs af analyseværktøjer og diverse rapporter.
  • Blive rutineret bruger af vores dataværktøjspakke Nielsen Discover, Kauza samt andre analyseværktøjer og med tiden håndtere forespørgsler fra marketing og salg.
  • Administrere og vedligeholde stamdata til diverse dataværktøjer.


Om dig

Vi søger en studerende, der er i gang med 2. eller 3. år af en relevant bacheloruddannelse, har interesse for tal og analyse, og som allerede nu har planer om at fortsætte på en kandidat i forlængelse af din bachelor.


Vi lægger herudover vægt på, at du:

  • Har en analytisk og datadrevet tilgang til dit arbejde.
  • Evner at tilgå dine opgaver med fordybelse og opmærksomhed på detaljer.
  • Trives med at planlægge og udføre egne opgaver.
  • Evner at skabe struktur i dine arbejdsopgaver og på din arbejdsfordeling.
  • Behersker engelsk i tale og skrift ubesværet.


Vi sikrer grundig introduktion og opgaveoverdragelse, så du kan føle dig godt klædt på til at varetage opgaverne.


Vi tilbyder

Hos Lavazza Denmark er vi engageret omkring det, vi laver. Som en organisation i udvikling, arbejder vi hver dag fokuseret på at udvikle forretningen og støtte hinanden. Vi tror på, at positive resultater skabes af mennesker, der trives – og vi prioriterer vi din personlige og faglige udvikling som en integreret del af vores strategi og hverdag.


Vi har fokus på en inkluderende kultur, hvor alle oplever, at vores værdier: Authenticity, Passion for Excellence, Responsibility og Inventiveness leves i dagligdagen.

Hos os får du mulighed for at være med til at udvikle en virksomhed, hvor engagement, kvalitet og fremdrift går hånd i hånd.


Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål til stillingen, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte Senior Research Manager, Birgitte Hein på +45 50 59 91 10.


Vi beder dig sende din ansøgning, CV samt dokumentation for igangværende uddannelse. Vi behandler ansøgningerne løbende, og besætter stillingen, når vi har den rette kandidat.


Om os

Lavazza Denmark er en del af Lavazza Group, der har produceret ægte italiensk kaffe i 130 år. Luigi Lavazza S.P.A. er blandt verdens førende kaffeleverandører, med en årlig omsætning på € 3.1 milliarder og mere end 5.500 dedikerede medarbejdere, der deler Lavazza passionen for at tilbyde forbrugerne den ypperste kaffekvalitet og kaffenydelse.


Lavazza Denmark er beliggende i Fredericia. Vi er 88 medarbejdere i Lavazza Denmark, som er hovedkontor for de nordiske organisationer.


Lavazza er en familiejet koncern, etableret i 1895, med hovedsæde i Torino i Italien. Lavazza er Italiens førende kaffemærke. Virksomhedens kaffeprodukter nydes endvidere i mere end 90 lande verden over. www.merrild.dk – www.lavazza.dk
