Title: Data Engineer
Place: Aalborg, North Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
We’re looking for a skilled Data Engineer to join our Device Health team at Trackunit. In this role, you’ll work closely with your team and other teams to make sure that our customers knows the exact health of their devices, helping them stay connected at all time.

From organizing data to finding new ways of detecting device health issues to collaborating across teams in engineering, product and sales, you will be in the front seat of one of the most important and high impact areas when it comes to helping our customers and their fleets staying connected.

Whether you prefer working from one of our hubs in Copenhagen, Kolding, Aarhus, or Aalborg, or remotely from anywhere in Denmark, we offer a flexible setup designed for high-impact collaboration.

If you’re ready to bring your technical expertise to a role that has very high impact in construction, this is your chance to build something meaningful in one of the world’s most essential industries!

What’s in it for you?


A place in an agile growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees.
International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.
We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.
Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.
Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.


Who are you ideally?


You bring a strong background in data engineering, data warehousing, or data science/AI/ML.
You have hands-on experience with the Databricks platform and are familiar with its key features and ecosystem.
You are proficient in Python, SQL and Spark, and can apply them effectively in real-world data scenarios.
You excel at organizing data for increasing quality, structure and business readiness.
Experience or understanding of the construction and off-highway vehicle industry is a strong plus.


Don't meet every single requirement? No worries - this is what we're looking for ideally, but if you're particularly drawn to this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Primary Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and data models that power device health insights across the Trackunit platform.
Take ownership of structuring, cleaning, and organizing complex IoT and telematics data to ensure high data quality, reliability, and business readiness.
Collaborate closely with the team’s Data Scientist to operationalize analytics models, experiments, and algorithms into robust, production-grade data solutions.
Explore large-scale device datasets to uncover patterns, anomalies, and new opportunities for detecting and preventing device health issues-
Contribute to the continuous improvement of our data architecture, tooling, and best practices. Support the development of new device health use cases that increase uptime, reliability, and value for our customers’ fleets.


Our hiring processDon't waste your time on writing the best possible cover letter for the job. We want you to create an impact that matters, and that's not in the cover letter.


Initial phone conversation with a colleague from our P&T team, Anders.
Interview with Stefan Højrup, your potential new leader, and relevant people from the team, so you’ll get to know them and the team better.
Assignment-specific interview. We want you to get an insight into some of the concrete work tasks or projects related to the role. You will be given a case to prepare prior to the interview, and at the interview, you will present the case to relevant colleagues from across Trackunit, who you’ll also work closely with in the job.
If needed, throughout the process we will obtain references from former employers and do background checks for level specific, if you have not provided these yourself.
Offer presentation and walk-through. We’re lucky to have you!


Coming Together To Connect Construction We’re committed to construction - one of the largest industries in the world. Over the past two decades, Trackunit has been pioneering technological progress within construction. Today, we are not only a leading IoT provider but a thought leader, supporting and shaping the agenda for an entire industry on a global scale.

We believe in taking a people approach in everything we do. Being human-centric is not restricted to our products – it’s a way of life at Trackunit. We’re proud to be a truly global team. Our colleagues get together in hubs spread across the globe, but we embrace the idea of working remotely and in environments that inspire you. Everything we do, we do it to eliminate downtime and build the most useful industry for the world.

The question is: Are you in?

Title: Senior Quantitative Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
On behalf of my client, I am currently recruiting for a Flexibility Lead in Copenhagen.




This role will be focused on the energy trading domain, so someone who's has real domain knowledge that wants a new challenge would be a great match.




Quantitative development & optimisation

Build and maintain flexibility trading algorithms for renewable, hybrid and BESS assets, maximising revenue across energy, ancillary services, and capacity markets in Denmark (first, then Germany, then expanding to wider Europe.
Design, back-test, implement in code and continuously improve trading strategies for intraday, imbalance, and frequency response markets.
Build revenue stacking models that quantify and optimise the interplay between co-located revenue streams, including hybrid wind/solar-plus-storage configurations.
Design optimisation models in unison with hybrid PPA offerings, ensuring max value for developers and corporates.
Be responsible and accountable for the realized performance of our flexibility portfolio




Overall, we’d expect you’d spend around 70-80% on the quant development side and 20-30% on the commercial side, being mostly involved in key touchpoints in the commercial dialogue. This might change as we grow our portfolio and will be dependent on the needs of the business.




If this sounds like you please apply directly.

Title: Internship - Data Scientist
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Apply AI to healthcare challenges that matter. Join our Innovation team in Aarhus for a hands-on internship where you'll develop machine learning solutions that support clinical decision-making for healthcare professionals worldwide.

Expected start:
August 2026 
The internship 
You'll work on real AI challenges in healthcare, contributing to machine learning models that help clinicians make better decisions. From data exploration to model deployment, you'll experience the full data science workflow and see your work integrated into products that improve patient care across hospitals and communities.

Your key responsibilities include:


Developing and experimenting with ML/AI models for healthcare products, working with areas such as natural language processing, speech recognition, or anomaly detection
Assisting in prompt engineering and working with large language models (LLMs)
Preprocessing, analysing, and visualising healthcare data
Contributing to model evaluation, documentation, and testing
Collaborating with data scientists, architects, product managers, and domain experts
Participating in technical sparring sessions and code reviews
Bringing fresh ideas and perspectives to the team's innovation efforts



Join our Innovation team in Healthcare
Our Healthcare Innovation team sits at the heart of Systematics' mission to support healthcare professionals in critical environments. We develop AI solutions that simplify decision-making for users of our Columna platform worldwide. You'll join six colleagues - data scientists, architects and developers - who are eager to share their knowledge and explore what AI can achieve in healthcare. The team thrives on informal communication, mutual trust and genuine curiosity. We also enjoy table tennis breaks, plenty of coffee and laughs, and occasional matches on our padel court.

Your Qualifications
You are pursuing a BSc or MSc in Data Science, Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Cognitive Science, Statistics, or similar, and you have:


Practical experience with Python and machine learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Transformers)
Understanding of LLMs and prompt engineering – you have experimented with large language models and understand their potential applications
Experience developing or implementing machine learning models – you have built models from data preprocessing through to evaluation
Knowledge of Docker and FastAPI
You understand data handling, model evaluation, the visualisation of data-driven solutions and the development of best practices.
Appreciate teamwork and has good communication skills
You speak and write English fluently as it is our corporate language. It is also an advantage if you can speak Danish, but this is not a requirement. 



The internship is a full-time position lasting five months, commencing on 17 August 2026. To be eligible, you must be enrolled in a Danish study program and be based in Denmark during the internship period. The position is unpaid; however, if permitted by your academic programme, you will receive a monthly token of appreciation during your internship. 

Life as an Intern
Interns are valued members of our teams. From day one, you will be assigned a dedicated tutor or mentor who will guide your development and provide ongoing support. You will work alongside experienced colleagues eager to share their knowledge, while being entrusted with real responsibility early in your journey. 

We believe in delivering high-quality solutions while maintaining a positive work environment – whether through shared laughter over coffee, engaging discussions, or meaningful connections with colleagues. Beyond your immediate team, you will join our intern network, where you can build relationships and participate in social events with other interns across the organization. 

Please note that you must be able to present a clean criminal record and have resided in a NATO and/or EU country for the past seven years, as we work with security-classified projects. Read more here. 

Welcome to Systematic Aarhus 
At our Aarhus office, you can look forward to joining our Employees Club, which offers nearly 100 events annually of both social and professional nature. You will also benefit from a range of on-site amenities, including physiotherapist-led massages, an in-house hairdresser, sports clubs, game tables, and even our own padel court. Our canteen is a highlight, serving a daily selection of delicious and healthy dishes. Beyond the office, you will enjoy benefits such as free ARoS +1 access and various shopping discounts. 

Start your development here 
We will be assessing applications on an ongoing basis as we wish to find our new intern as soon as possible, so click Apply Now and send us your CV, Cover Letter and exam certificates. If you have any questions, you are welcome to contact us on tel. +45 8943 2000 and ask for Tina Rasmussen, Senior Program Manager in Healthcare Operations. 

Get in touch

Mads Grøn Binder

Junior Consultant, People Attraction

Title: Sports Data Collector (Football) - Odense, Denmark
Place: Odense, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Sports Data Collector (Sports Scout)

Independent Contractor | Paid per game


Please note: All CVs are required to be submitted in English.


Love sports? Turn your passion into paid experience.

Genius Sports is a global sports technology company powering official live data for the world's biggest leagues, federations, and media platforms.

We're recruiting Sports Data Collector (Sports Scout) to attend live sporting events and collect real-time, play-by-play data using our proprietary software.

What you'll do


Attend live sports events in your local area from Superisligaen and lower profile competitions
Record accurate, real-time game data (scores, plays, key events)
Use mobile technology in a fast-paced, live environment


What we're looking for


Strong knowledge of the sport
Reliable, detail-oriented, and organized
Availability on evenings and weekends
18+ years old


What we offer


Fixed payment per game - 70€
Flexible, game-based work
Training and testing provided
Opportunity to increase earnings based on performance


Interested?

Tell us a bit about yourself and we'll be in touch.

👉 Learn more about our Statisticians Network:

https://www.geniussports.com/careers/statisticians-network/

Genius Sports is an equal opportunities employer.

Title: Quantitative Analyst - Forward Power
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Are you an experienced Quantitative Analyst who enjoys turning data, models and ideas into real impact?




We’re looking for a Quantitative Analyst to join our Forward Power team and work at the intersection of quantitative modelling and power trading.




In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with traders to develop and improve quantitative models, analytics and decision-support tools across forward power markets. You’ll work hands-on with data and production-grade code, contributing throughout the full lifecycle from idea to live implementation in trading.




Our Forward Power team has a clear ambition to become a leading force in the European power markets, and we’re looking for a quantitative profile who shares that ambition. If you’re eager to push boundaries and join a team of experienced and driven colleagues, this is your chance.




Let’s dive into the details!




Your tasks will include

Work closely with traders to develop and refine quantitative models that support profitable trading strategies
Help build and develop stack models for CWE markets
Continuously seek improvements to workflows, analytics, risk tools, and business processes
Conduct ad hoc quick analysis to support trading, risk, and commercial decision-making
Design, develop, and maintain analytic and operational views used for monitoring strategies and market dynamics for trading
Support the development and maintenance of Python-based models
Contribute directly to advancing and scaling our data infrastructure




What we're looking for

Minimum 2 years of experience as a Quantitative Analyst, preferably within Forward Power or Day-Ahead
Hold a Master within a quantitative field
You have strong quantitative, analytical, and problem-solving skills
You have great Python knowledge and an eagerness to code. Experience with other code languages is considered an advantage
You’re a strong team player with a pragmatic, execution-focused mindset
You’re driven, take ownership, and are willing to challenge the status quo to create impact




What we offer

A young, dynamic, ambitious and fun work environment
Extensive opportunities for personal development and career growth. You will be given responsibility from day one and receive close sparring with skilled colleagues
The opportunity to contribute and make your mark on a young company’s development
A competitive salary package

Are you an experienced Quantitative Analyst who enjoys turning data, models and ideas into real impact?


We’re looking for a Quantitative Analyst to join our Forward Power team and work at the intersection of quantitative modelling and power trading.


In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with traders to develop and improve quantitative models, analytics and decision-support tools across forward power markets. You’ll work hands-on with data and production-grade code, contributing throughout the full lifecycle from idea to live implementation in trading.


Our Forward Power team has a clear ambition to become a leading force in the European power markets, and we’re looking for a quantitative profile who shares that ambition. If you’re eager to push boundaries and join a team of experienced and driven colleagues, this is your chance.


Let’s dive into the details!


Your tasks will include

  • Work closely with traders to develop and refine quantitative models that support profitable trading strategies
  • Help build and develop stack models for CWE markets
  • Continuously seek improvements to workflows, analytics, risk tools, and business processes
  • Conduct ad hoc quick analysis to support trading, risk, and commercial decision-making
  • Design, develop, and maintain analytic and operational views used for monitoring strategies and market dynamics for trading
  • Support the development and maintenance of Python-based models
  • Contribute directly to advancing and scaling our data infrastructure


What we're looking for

  • Minimum 2 years of experience as a Quantitative Analyst, preferably within Forward Power or Day-Ahead
  • Hold a Master within a quantitative field
  • You have strong quantitative, analytical, and problem-solving skills
  • You have great Python knowledge and an eagerness to code. Experience with other code languages is considered an advantage
  • You’re a strong team player with a pragmatic, execution-focused mindset
  • You’re driven, take ownership, and are willing to challenge the status quo to create impact


What we offer

  • A young, dynamic, ambitious and fun work environment
  • Extensive opportunities for personal development and career growth. You will be given responsibility from day one and receive close sparring with skilled colleagues
  • The opportunity to contribute and make your mark on a young company’s development
  • A competitive salary package
  • A fun and social work environment with events such as Friday bars, poker tournaments, summer and Christmas parties etc. throughout the year. We also offer weekly sports activities to boost team spirit, such as our Nitor-running club!
  • Delicious lunch arrangement with food from Meyers in our own canteen
  • Brand new office facilities located in the heart of Aarhus, right next to Dokk1 and Letbanen


Expected start date: As soon as possible

Please note that we review applications on a rolling basis and may close the position early if we find the right candidate.



About Nitor Energy

Nitor Energy is a fast-growing energy trading company, that has swiftly transitioned from start-up to scale-up within just a few years. We’re experiencing rapid growth, offering limitless opportunities for learning and development. We’re proud of who we are and excited about what we’re building for the future together. Our employees thrive on challenges and are motivated by the potential that lies ahead.


Our unique culture integrates traders, coders, and analysts into cohesive teams, creating a well-rounded approach to our success. We believe in a balanced work-life dynamic and foster a vibrant, engaging atmosphere where our employees can excel in every aspect.

Title: Data Scientist (Python & SQL) - Freelance AI Trainer
Place: Denmark
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture, allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Analytics Engineer to build and shape Spirii's analytics layer and data reliability practices.

Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll implement dbt standards, data models, tests, and documentation — turning raw data in Amazon Redshift into trusted, well-governed data products across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to define best practices from day one, lay the foundations for scalable transformations and consistent metrics, and help build Spirii's data culture from the ground up.

Your Responsibilities

Data Modelling & dbt


Help build and shape our dbt project: structure, conventions, CI practices, environments, and release workflows.
Design and implement analytics-ready data models (e.g., staging/intermediate/marts, fact/dimension modelling) that enable self-serve analytics and reliable reporting.


Data Quality & Governance


Define and implement data quality practices together with domain teams: dbt tests, freshness checks, anomaly detection, and data contracts.
Improve data documentation, lineage, and discoverability.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to ensure analytics requirements are addressed upstream (events, schemas, identifiers, change management).
Collaborate with Finance and business teams to standardize metrics and eliminate inconsistent metric definitions across dashboards.
Contribute to a strong data culture in Spirii: patterns, templates, reviews, enablement, and data literacy.


Platform & Infrastructure


Support and evolve our ELT foundations: help improve ingestion reliability, orchestration, monitoring, and incident response (in collaboration with Engineering/Data Lead).
Optimize model performance and cost in Redshift.


About You


You care deeply about data correctness and building systems humans can trust.
You're comfortable being hands-on: you ship models, tests, and improvements yourself.
You think of data models as products with consumers — you care about who uses them, for what purpose, and whether they can be trusted over time.
You collaborate well across teams and can drive alignment on definitions and ownership.
You're excited to help define what makes a metric canonical and move away from metrics defined ad-hoc in dashboards.
You're pragmatic: you know when to implement a robust standard and when to keep it lean.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or software engineering with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and demonstrated experience building analytics models.
Solid dbt experience (core patterns, project structuring, tests, documentation, and deployment practices).
Experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake also relevant).
Comfortable with at least one programming language used in data work (Python preferred).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts and some hands-on experience improving pipelines, reliability, or orchestration.
Familiar with data governance concepts (access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices).
Experience working with BI tools and stakeholders to define consistent metrics.


Nice to Have


Experience setting up data observability tooling (or implementing monitoring patterns around dbt runs, freshness, volume, distribution changes).
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with IaC (Terraform/CDK), and/or CI/CD pipelines for data workflows.
Machine learning experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but Fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company, however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as development of future transport- and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability

Title: Senior Business Analyst – Data, Insights & AI
Place: Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Senior Business Analyst in Falck’s Data, Insights & AI department, you are the key link between the business and our technology. You turn complex business challenges into data- and AI-enabled solutions, drive discovery and value framing, and ensure that what we build delivers measurable impact for Falck’s customers, patients, and operations.

You will work across Business Units and teams, shaping how we use data, analytics, automation, and AI to advance sustainable healthcare and emergency response.

We are looking for a T-shaped profile – someone with deep expertise in business analysis who is also continuously expanding their skills across data, analytics, and AI as technologies and ways of working evolve. You will join a growing community of 60+ data and AI professionals in Copenhagen and Warsaw. Your focus is to find the right problems, define the right solutions, and ensure they are adopted and deliver value.

Your Main Responsibilities Will Involve


Identifying and framing high-impact business problems where data, analytics, automation, and AI can create strategic value as well as looking beyond the initial request to understand the underlying business need, challenge assumptions, and reframe problems to maximize value.
Building ROI-based business cases: articulate expected costs, benefits, and risks; quantify value (e.g., revenue uplift, cost savings, efficiency, quality, experience).
Acting as a trusted business partner to senior stakeholders across multiple business units.
Facilitating workshops, interviews, and working sessions to align on problems, priorities, and solution options.
Translating business problems into clear, testable requirements (epics, user stories, acceptance criteria) plus translate business concepts into logical and conceptual data models, defining how entities, relationships, measures, and KPIs should be represented in the data platform.
Collaborating daily with Engineers to validate feasibility and data availability, co-design solution approaches, and refine requirements as learning emerges.
Owning the business side of the solution lifecycle – from discovery to adoption: requirements and design, testing and validation, business readiness and go-live, and post-launch optimization.
Leading or coordinating User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including designing test cases that match real-world scenarios and edge cases.
Mentoring and support more junior team members, especially colleagues working with business analysis and insights, sharing best practices and providing feedback on complex challenges.
Being recognized as a go-to domain expert in your business area and share lessons learned across teams and projects.


About youExperience & track record


Several years of experience as a Business Analyst preferably in data, analytics, digital, or AI-driven environments.
Proven track record of delivering measurable business outcomes through applied analytics, automation, or AI initiatives.
Demonstrated experience in end-to-end delivery – from discovery and business case, through requirements and solution design, to testing, deployment, adoption, and benefit tracking.
Solid experience with stakeholder management at senior levels, including navigating conflicting priorities and driving alignment.
Strong problem-structuring and problem-solving skills.
Ability to break down complex challenges into clear, prioritized increments.
Experience writing high-quality user stories, use cases, and acceptance criteria.
Ability to convert data into insight narratives that drive decisions.
Familiarity with AI and ML concepts
Work with technical teams on data modelling approaches, helping to shape how business concepts are implemented in our data lakehouse architecture and in dimensional models so analytics and insights are trustworthy and easy to consume.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken; ability to make complex and “black box” topics understandable and actionable.
Strong facilitation skills for workshops, interviews, and cross-functional sessions.
Curious and continuously learning – especially about AI, data, and customer behaviour.
Collaborative – you work in cross-functional groups and share knowledge openly.
Structured and able to manage multiple parallel initiatives without losing sight of detail or big picture.


About us

Falck’s Data, Insights & AI department brings together experts in data engineering, analytics, AI, automation, and business partnering to support a global healthcare and emergency services organisation. With 60+ professionals in Copenhagen and Warsaw, we combine deep technical expertise with strong business understanding to build data and AI solutions that are reliable, scalable, and actually used.

We work closely across the business and with central technology teams, including a mission-critical Data Platform team that provides shared standards and frameworks to accelerate delivery. We invest in our people, encourage T-shaped development, and foster a culture of care, trust, and collaboration – turning care into action through data and AI.

About Us

At Falck we build on a legacy of more than one hundred years in healthcare and emergency services. With a presence in 25 countries, our 25,000 skilled professionals delivered more than 9.5 million services last year. We are committed to care, and to care for others, we need to take care of ourselves. That is why we continuously promote engagement, diversity, health, and safety among our employees. Every day, we work to advance sustainable healthcare. And every day, we turn care into action.

About The Team

Our global functions are integral to enabling the organisation to live our purpose. Through dedication and expertise , these teams support Falck in delivering care and making a meaningful difference in people's lives. If you are motivated by contributing to something greater, Falck is the place to bring care into your career .

Title: UX & Insights Intern (Web Analyst)
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Are you looking to gain hands-on experience with web analytics and digital insights in an international retail organisation? At JYSK Head Office in Aarhus, you will join a growing UX & Insights department where data and usability play a key role in improving our online performance across markets.




As a Web Analyst Intern, you will work closely with experienced specialists and gain practical insight into how online data supports decision-making and optimisation in a large international business.




WHAT WE OFFER YOU

This internship gives you the opportunity to develop your analytical skills and apply your theoretical knowledge in practice within web analytics.




As a Web Analyst Intern, you will work with data and insights that support optimisation of our digital customer journey and online performance.




You will become part of an informal and professional working environment at our Head Office in Aarhus, where collaboration, clear priorities and responsibility go hand in hand.




Furthermore, we offer you:

Hands-on experience with web analytics tools, primarily Google Analytics 4
Close sparring and guidance from experienced web analytics specialists
The opportunity to work independently with defined tasks and analyses
Exposure to data-driven decision-making in an international set-up
An internship that supports both professional learning and personal development




WHAT YOUR NEXT INTERNSHIP WILL BE ABOUT

Your tasks and responsibilities in the Internship include:

Analysing user behaviour and traffic data to support conversion optimisation
Preparing reports that create clear and actionable insights
Collaborating with internal stakeholders on data-related tasks
Identifying improvement opportunities based on digital performance data
Supporting ongoing development of tracking and insight frameworks




You can read more about our department here.




WHAT SHOULD YOU BRING

We are looking for a new Intern who:

Is currently studying E-commerce, IT, Business Analytics or a related field
Has an interest in web analytics and digital user behaviour, especially GA4
Works independently and takes responsibility for own tasks
Has a structured and detail-oriented approach
Has experience with or interest in tools such as Google Tag Manager, GA4 and Excel
Communicates fluently in written and spoken English




Additional Information

IS THIS YOUR NEXT OPPORTUNITY? Apply today!

If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Senior Web Analyst, Lasse Bojesen at +45 5133 6888.




We kindly ask you to only attach your CV. With us, there is no need to write a traditional cover letter when applying for an Internship.

Title: Landbrugsmaskinmekaniker
Place: Glamsbjerg, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Bliv en del af et stærkt værkstedsteam med frihed, faglighed og godt humør

Er du typen, der kan lide at skrue, fejlsøge og få store maskiner til at spille?

Og vil du gerne have et job, hvor du bliver værdsat, har frihed i hverdagen – og kollegaer der hjælper hinanden?

Så har vi jobbet til dig.




Hos Glamsbjerg Maskincenter søger vi en landbrugsmaskinmekaniker, der vil være med til at holde traktorer, mejetærskere og entreprenørmaskiner kørende – både på værkstedet og ude hos kunderne.

Du bliver en del af et team på 27 medarbejdere, hvor vi arbejder i en flad organisation med kort vej fra idé til handling. Vi tager fagligheden seriøst – men der skal også være plads til et grin i hverdagen.


Dine opgaver bliver at:
Fejlsøge, reparere og servicere landbrugs- og entreprenørmaskiner
Klargøre nye og brugte maskiner til levering og drift
Diagnosticere og løse tekniske udfordringer
Køre ud til kunder og hjælpe, når maskinerne driller
Bidrage til et værksted med godt samarbejde og høj faglighed

Hvad vi forestiller os:
Er uddannet landbrugsmaskinmekaniker
Kan arbejde selvstændigt, men trives i et team
Har kørekort og lyst til serviceopgaver ude hos kunder
Er løsningsorienteret og bevarer humøret, også når en bolt nægter at slippe

Hvad vi tilbyder dig:
En lønpakke, der matcher dine kvalifikationer
Frihed under ansvar og mulighed for at forme din egen arbejdsdag
Mulighed for kurser og efteruddannelse
Et værksted med gode faciliteter og ordentligt grej
Et fællesskab med højt humør og god stemning
En arbejdsplads, hvor vi tager arbejdet seriøst – men ikke os selv!

Hvis du er klar, så er vi klar?

Send din ansøgning og CV til værkfører Ulrik Bøgelund på reservedele@gmc-fyn.dk 

Vi holder samtaler løbende, så send gerne din ansøgning hurtigst muligt.

Har du spørgsmål? Ring på 40989813 – vi tager gerne en snak.

Title: Quantitative Researcher
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Quantitative Researcher




Are you a quantitatively minded problem-solver who enjoys turning complex financial data into robust, systematic strategies? Do you combine strong mathematical thinking with practical programming skills — and have you already applied them in a financial markets context?




A leading quantitative investment team is looking for a Quantitative Researcher to help design, develop, and improve equity statistical arbitrage strategies. This role sits at the intersection of research and development — requiring both strong quantitative reasoning and the ability to build reliable research tools and models in code.




This is an opportunity to work in a collaborative, intellectually rigorous environment where ideas are tested quickly and engineering quality matters.




What You'll Do:

📊 Develop Quantitative Strategies – Research and implement systematic equity statistical arbitrage models using large financial datasets.

🧪 Experiment and Iterate – Design research experiments, evaluate signals, and refine strategies through rigorous analysis and testing.

💻 Build Research Tools – Write clean, efficient Python code to process data, develop models, and support the research pipeline.

📈 Work with Financial Data – Analyse complex datasets to identify patterns, inefficiencies, and opportunities in financial markets — particularly fixed income and equity instruments.

🤝 Collaborate with Engineers and Researchers – Work closely with a small team of technically strong colleagues to translate ideas into production-ready strategies.




The Team:

You'll join a lean quantitative research group focused on systematic trading strategies. The environment is highly technical but pragmatic — ideas are judged by evidence, and researchers are expected to both design models and build the tools needed to test them. The team values people who combine theoretical insight with the ability to implement their ideas in code.




What They're Looking For:

Strong quantitative background (e.g. Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or similar)
Experience applying quantitative or ML methods to financial data — whether in systematic trading, derivatives, risk modelling, or a related area of financial markets
Strong Python skills and experience working with large datasets
Evidence of practical software development experience
Ability to move between theory and implementation
This is not a role where financial markets knowledge can be learned on the job — some prior exposure to financial instruments or trading environments is expected




You may come from industry or academia, but should be able to point to concrete experience working with financial data or within a financial markets context.




What They Offer:

Highly competitive compensation with strong bonus potential
Pension, healthcare, and comprehensive benefits
A collaborative environment with direct impact on live trading strategies
The opportunity to work on challenging quantitative problems with high-quality data and infrastructure




💰 Base salary is 60-90 Danish Krona per month (€120K – €150K equivalent per year) and benefits include bonus

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data Engineer




On behalf of our client we are recruiting a Data Engineer for a temp position, you will play a hands-on role in building, shaping, and operating data products that are reliable, well‑structured, and easy for stakeholders to consume. You will collaborate closely with local data teams, BI specialists, business partners, and platform teams to deliver scalable, source‑aligned data products and consumer‑ready datasets.

This role suits someone who enjoys creating long‑lasting solutions: robust pipelines, clear models, governance‑by‑design, and reusable patterns that elevate the overall data ecosystem.




Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain data pipelines and transformations using DBT, Snowflake, SQL, and Python.
Develop scalable, well‑structured data models to support BI, analytics, and data activation use cases.
Contribute to data product standards, including testing, documentation, lineage, and versioning.
Enable and accelerate local data teams through hands‑on coaching and platform guidance.
Collaborate with BI specialists and stakeholders to clarify requirements and translate them into usable data outputs and solution architectures.
Participate in design reviews and influence architectural decisions within the data engineering domain.




Contract Details

Duration: 9 months
Start: Latest 1 May
Location: Copenhagen — 3 days onsite per week

Title: Project Coordinator
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
As a global leader in facilities services we connect people and places to make the world work better. Whether directly or indirectly, you’ll play a vital role in supporting our placemakers in delivering exceptional workplace experiences for our customers. Together, we make space for people and businesses to thrive.

Location: Copenhagen, Denmark

Language: English

Main purpose of the position

The project coordinator will be part of the Chief People & Technology (CPTO) Office team who is supporting the agenda of the Group Chief People & Technology Officer. In this role, you will be instrumental in increasing efficiency, alignment and consistency by planning, coordination and preparation of planned and ad-hoc day-to-day meetings and tasks of the CPTO Office. In addition, you will work closely with the European Area People & Culture Directors in driving the area and country P&C agendas and ensure knowledge-sharing across countries and ISS Group. As a project coordinator in the CPTO Office you will manage a broad range of stakeholders and get exposure to various functions and markets of the business.

What You’ll Do


Provide day-to-day support to Head of People & Technology Office with managing tasks on the agenda of the People & Culture Senior Leadership Team
Work closely with P&C Director of Austria and Finland in driving area P&C boards of Central & Southern Europe and Northern Europe
Project manage across countries related to strategic priorities
Facilitate communication across Europe allowing best practices to flourish, ideas to be shared, and challenges solved collectively
Organising area P&C leadership activities in Regional Boards as well as across countries including conferences, large meetings, etc.
Provide administrative and shared services support to P&C Senior Leadership centre of excellence teams


Who You’ll Work With


Head of CPTO Office
P&C Director, Austria and Central & Southern European P&C board
P&C Director, Finland and Northern European P&C board
P&C Senior Leadership Team


Key Qualifications


Min. 2 years of experience with project management and diary and meeting planning
Strong interpersonal skills and experience with navigating diverse and complex stakeholder groups
Strong communication skills, written and verbal
Attentive to details while having the ability to uphold a steady work pace
Ability to work structured and confidently on multiple projects and tasks at the same time
Business acumen and preferably experience from working in an international organisation.


Personal Skills You Excel


Structured mindset
Collaborative yet self-driven
Willing to learn


Success Criteria


Ability to work independently, consistently and reliably with task-solving and project coordination across CPTO Office and Area P&C agendas.
Successfully support more efficient, aligned and consistent working processes.
Efficiently provide administrative support to the wider P&C senior leadership team.


Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.

ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.

How You’ll Apply

Apply directly via the link on this page by submitting a cover letter, CV and other relevant documents for the position you are applying for. We look forward to receiving your application as soon as possible.

ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: Implementation Specialist
Place: Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Title: Implementation Specialist

Location: Denmark 🇩🇰

Company: Meril Endo Surgery




🌟 About the Role

Meril Endo Surgery is looking for a highly driven and clinically skilled Implementation Specialist to lead the adoption of advanced surgical solutions across hospitals in Denmark.

This is a high-impact, field-based role where you will work closely with surgeons and medical teams in real-time surgical environments, ensuring seamless integration of innovative technologies into clinical practice and directly contributing to improved patient outcomes.

🔍 Key Responsibilities

Drive end-to-end implementation of surgical solutions across hospitals
Provide hands-on support in Operation Theatres (OT) during procedures
Deliver clinical training and product demonstrations to surgeons and OT staff
Collaborate with internal teams for smooth onboarding and adoption
Act as a clinical expert for Obs/Gynae and General Surgery applications
Troubleshoot real-time challenges during procedures
Build strong relationships with healthcare professionals
Ensure adherence to clinical protocols and quality standards

👤 Ideal Candidate Profile

Strong exposure to Obs / Gynae / General Surgery
Background in surgical, paramedical, or clinical support roles preferred
Comfortable working in fast-paced hospital environments
Willing to travel extensively within Denmark
Strong communication, stakeholder management, and problem-solving skills
Ability to work independently in a field-driven role

💼 Why This Opportunity Stands Out

Be part of a fast-growing global medical device organization
Work at the forefront of surgical innovation
Make a direct impact on patient outcomes
Competitive compensation and benefits
Collaborate with leading surgeons and healthcare institutions

📌 Additional Insights

Field-intensive, hospital-based role requiring strong on-ground presence
Focus on implementation excellence (not a sales-driven role)
Ideal for professionals who thrive in hands-on clinical environments

Title: Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Hvem er vi?

GO-Pen ApS er en scale-up-virksomhed i medicinsk udstyrs branchen. Vi har udviklet en innovativ insulinpen, der er designet til at være tilgængelig for alle. Produktet er nu blevet godkendt af FDA (food and drug administration i USA) og har fået CE mærkning, og næste skridt er at få produktet ud på markederne. Vi er et lille, agilt hold, der arbejder passioneret på at hæve standarden for insulinadministration for de mest udfordrede diabetikere i verden. Hos os bliver du en del af et uformelt miljø med en flad struktur og en stærk fælles tro på vores produkt vil ændre livet for så mange som muligt af de + 20 millioner, der i dag har diabetes, men som kun har råd til at tage insulin med en sprøjte.

Hvad kommer du til at lave?

Din rolle vil primært være relateret til vores kommercielle strategier og markedsadgang. Dine specifikke ansvarsområder vil spænde bredt fra dybdegående markedsanalyser til stakeholder engagement og kortlægning af potentielle partnere. Du vil arbejde direkte under ledelsen, hvilket giver dig en unik og spændende indsigt i Danmarks scale-up / start-up-miljø samt førstehåndserfaring med at bygge en virksomhed op. Din indsats med at identificere vækstmuligheder og validere vores strategi vil direkte understøtte vores evne til at træffe de rigtige beslutninger og skalere forretningen.

Hvem er du?

Du er analytisk skarp, og du kan lide komplekse problemstillinger. Du har kompetencer som er anvendelige i medicinal- og medtech-industrien. Det er et stort plus, hvis du har en naturlig interesse for sundhedsøkonomi og markedsdynamikker. Vigtigst af alt har du et højt personligt drive, arbejder proaktivt og formår selvstændigt at tage ejerskab over dine opgaver.

Detaljer

Vores kontor er placeret i Lyngby, og stillingen er normeret til 15 - 20 timer om ugen. Vi ved, at dit studie har høj prioritet. Derfor vil der også være stor forståelse og fleksibilitet i dine eksamensperioder, så du kan balancere uddannelse og arbejde på en fornuftig måde.

Hvem er vi?

GO-Pen ApS er en scale-up-virksomhed i medicinsk udstyrs branchen. Vi har udviklet en innovativ insulinpen, der er designet til at være tilgængelig for alle. Produktet er nu blevet godkendt af FDA (food and drug administration i USA) og har fået CE mærkning, og næste skridt er at få produktet ud på markederne. Title: IT Assistant (Student Worker)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Firmabeskrivelse

Location: Copenhagen (hybrid flexibility, 3–4 days in office)

Company: Coach Solutions A/S

Industry: Maritime SaaS | Data & Sustainability | High-Impact Tech

Help us keep our IT infrastructure reliable, secure, and frictionless.

At Coach Solutions, we build maritime software that helps global shipping companies sail smarter, greener, and more profitably. Now backed by Kongsberg Maritime, we're entering a new growth phase – scaling our commercial engine, sharpening our technical foundation, and ensuring our teams have the IT support they need to do their best work.

We're looking for an IT Assistant (Student Worker) who thrives on service, systems, and solving problems – someone who keeps devices running, access flowing, and IT friction out of the way so our teams can focus on building impact.

Your impact

You'll own the IT foundation end-to-end – managing devices, maintaining infrastructure, supporting employees, and ensuring security compliance across our growing organization.

Reporting to the CEO and working closely with colleagues across The Strategic Engine, The Commercial Engine, and The Technical Engine, you'll ensure that our IT operations are reliable, secure, and enable productivity rather than creating bottlenecks.

You'll be responsible for:


Managing all company devices: enrolling laptops in device management, applying security updates, enforcing policies, and handling the full device lifecycle
Maintaining office IT infrastructure: keeping our network, Wi-Fi, and meeting room equipment running smoothly
Managing SaaS licenses: assigning/revoking access, tracking costs, identifying optimization opportunities across Google, Microsoft, Adobe, JetBrains, and more
Providing first-line IT support: helping employees with access issues, troubleshooting problems, and protecting developer time from IT interruptions
Onboarding and offboarding employees: provisioning accounts, tools, access, devices; ensuring clean and complete offboarding with zero security gaps
Managing mobile devices: setting up company phones, enforcing security, supporting common mobile issues
Maintaining accurate IT documentation: tracking assets, licenses, procedures, and ensuring transparent cost and security reporting


What Makes This Role Exciting


You'll gain hands-on IT experience in a real SaaS tech company while studying
You'll own meaningful responsibilities and improving systems, optimizing costs, and shaping how IT supports the business
You'll work closely with leadership and experienced colleagues across the organization
You'll have freedom to create structure where it doesn't exist and improve processes that need it
You'll develop practical IT skills that complement your studies and build your professional foundation
You'll be part of a highly ambitious growth phase backed by Kongsberg Maritime – with opportunities to learn and grow


Who You Are

You're a student pursuing relevant IT education (or an adjacent field), service-minded, systematic, technically curious, and calm under pressure. You get energy from keeping things running smoothly and helping people work without IT friction.

You're studying:

IT, Computer Science, Network Administration, Systems Administration, or related field

And you:


Have basic IT troubleshooting skills across Windows, macOS, and mobile platforms
Are curious about device management and willing to learn MDM/MAM platforms quickly
Can communicate clearly with non-technical users and explain problems without jargon or condescension
Are highly systematic. You track assets, document procedures, and maintain clean records
Stay calm and service-minded even when dealing with frustrated users or urgent issues
Take proactive ownership, spotting problems before they escalate and improve processes as you go
Are comfortable with Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, or similar SaaS platforms (or eager to learn)
Can work 15–20 hours/week with flexibility around your study schedule


Nice-to-haves (but not required):


Previous IT support, helpdesk, or student assistant experience
Familiarity with basic networking concepts
IT certifications (CompTIA A+, Microsoft, Apple)


What You’ll Get


Real hands-on IT experience that complements your studies and builds your CV
A purpose-driven workplace at the intersection of tech, data, and sustainability
A leadership team that cares about structure, clarity, and measurable performance
A close-knit team in Nordhavn, with colleagues in Copenhagen and globally
A workplace with an informal environment where we actually like each other and help one another across the organization
A flexible, informal culture where trust, humor, and psychological safety matter
Flexible working hours that adapt to your study schedule and exam periods
Learning and development opportunities – gain skills that matter in your career
Great colleagues who'll support your growth and help you learn


Compensation & Benefits:


Competitive student worker salary
Flexible working hours (15–20 hours/week)
Holiday according to Danish holiday law
Employer-paid phone and home internet


About Coach Solutions A/S

We’re a maritime software company headquartered in Copenhagen with offices in India, Singapore, and Greece. We’re fully owned by Norwegian Kongsberg Maritime and create solutions that help the shipping industry operate more efficiently and sustainably.

Ready to build reliable IT operations while gaining real-world experience?

We’d love to meet you. Reach out and let’s build something meaningful together.

How To Apply

Send your CV and a few lines about why you're interested as soon as possible and no later than end of April. We'll interview candidates on a rolling basis. We look forward to meeting you – and perhaps welcoming you as the person who keeps our IT infrastructure reliable, secure, and frictionless while building valuable skills for your future career.

Title: Visual Merchandiser
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
ZARA Visual Commercial Copenhagen




Our commercials maximizing sales through effective analysis and are responsible for the image of the section. Have an excessive interest for design and details




About us




Zara offers the latest fashion trends for women, men and children. An international brand with stores in the main cities of the world and online. Our business model is centred in our customers, constantly adapting to their needs. We love what we do. Teamwork, passion, curiosity, diversity, sustainability, creativity and humility are our daily motivation. Does it sound like you? Maybe you are a Zara person.




How we imagine you




You are passionate, curious, motivated and dynamic, with something different to contribute. You are detail-oriented, creative and proactive and are looking for a challenging yet rewarding career in fashion. You are able to multitask between your key responsibilities. You love fashion and celebrate self-expression. You have excellent analysis, prioritization and organizational skills. At Inditex you will find more than a job.

What we expect from you

As a Commercial, you are responsible for the image and layout of your store, making sure the latest trends and best sellers are represented according to the company specifications, balancing between commerciality and image. Some of your main responsibilities will be:







Monitoring daily product replenishment and maintaining a high level of image standards
Analyzing sales rankings to promote the correct merchandise
Competitor analysis and product feedback
Motivate, encourage, develop and inspire your team




What we offer




Our internal talent is our greatest asset and we are proud of offering internal promotion programs where you will find opportunities to grow, e-learning and training programs - we never stop learning!




You will be rewarded with a competitive compensation package and you will also receive 25% discount to buy the latest trends in any of our Inditex brands available in your market.

We are committed to ensure that our recruitment processes are barrier free and as inclusive as possible to everyone. This includes making adjustments for people with disability or long-term conditions.

Title: Senior Data Specialist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you ready to help improve banking by building a solid, trusted data foundation that enables better customer experiences and smarter decisions across the bank? Join the Data Cluster, where data, technology, and collaboration come together to drive progress across the organization.

Join our team at the Customer Engagement Individuals Tribe:

The Personal Customers business unit in Danske Bank is undergoing a major transformation, heavily reliant on a sustainable, scalable, and secure data foundation. This foundation is a key enabler for reaching the bank’s Forward’28 strategic objectives, ensuring better banking experiences and more reliable products for our customers.

You will join the Customer Engagement Individuals (CEI) Tribe, where the customer is at the centre of everything we do. The tribe owns key customer-facing systems across Danske Bank’s channels and capabilities. We are a diverse group of approximately 160 colleagues spanning roles such as Data Analysts, Data Engineers, Business & Functional Specialists, Architects, UX Designers and more, working across the Nordics, Lithuania, and India.

Who are we looking for?

We imagine you are a collaborative and analytical professional who enjoys working in complex environments, bridging business needs and technical solutions, and creating clarity where others see ambiguity.

About the Role:

Danske Bank is seeking a Business & Functional Specialist to join one of our Data Foundation squads within the Customer Engagement Individuals Tribe. In this role, you will play a central part in enabling high-quality, trusted data across the Personal Customers domain, forming the backbone for next-generation banking services.

You will work at the intersection of business, data, and technology, translating complex business needs into clear and sustainable data specifications. By doing so, you will help commercial teams unlock value from data and ensure system owners can rely on consistent, compliant, and high-quality data for downstream use cases.

A normal workday for our business functional specialist will consist of working in cross collaboration with your team of data experts and business & system stakeholders ensuring the business requirements are translated properly into technical specifications to ensure sustainable delivery of data are across the bank. This will enable us to deliver on high quality customer products as well as enable us to become more digital as outlines in the Forward ’28 strategy. Also, it will consist of engaging with a wide range of stakeholders to serve both upstream and downstream business needs through data.

Danske Bank supports a high degree of workplace flexibility (our team is currently using a hybrid working model, where we work at least 3 days a week in the office.). Location is Copenhagen, Århus, Vilnius or Helsinki. No relocation is offered.

You will:


Work closely with business stakeholders, system owners, and data owners to ensure prioritised activities that support better customer experiences
Translate business logic and functional requirements into clear data specifications that support sustainable golden-source data models
Collaborate across the bank with both technical and non-technical stakeholders to help drive the bank-wide data agenda in line with the Forward’28 strategy
Enable downstream stakeholders to realise business value by ensuring timely and reliable data availability together with the team
Ensure thorough, coherent documentation and adherence to governance and compliance standards within the subject area
Contribute to the transformation of Danske Bank’s retail customer area to support the goals set out in Forward’28
Continuously learn and grow in the role, working to deliver a high-quality experience for customers
Help ensure alignment with regulatory, governance, and security frameworks, enabling end-to-end compliant, accessible, and secure data


About you:


Bachelor’s degree or higher within Business, Engineering, Natural/Life Sciences, Engineering or similar
Experience as a Business Analyst, Business & Functional Specialist, or in a similar role
Experience with security, compliance, risk, and financial services data is beneficial
Proficiencies in data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, or similar is beneficial
Analytical, able to manage shifting priorities, and comfortable working in uncertain and changing environments
A collaborative mindset with strong facilitation skills
For more senior profiles, strong organizational understanding and strategic thinking are key
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with fluency in spoken and written English


We offer:

Monthly salary range: this position can be located both in Lithuania, Finland and Denmark. If you are hired in Lithuania, the applicable salary range will be from 3920 to 5880 EUR monthly (based on your competencies relevant for the job).

Additionally, each Danske Bank employee receives employee benefits package which includes:


Growth opportunities: professional & supportive team, e-learnings, numerous development programs; (incl. professional certificates); 100+ professions for internal mobility opportunities.
Health & Well-being: a diverse, inclusive, work & life balance work environment; additional health insurance; mental well-being practices; partial psychologist counselling compensation; silence and sleep zones at the office; game rooms.
Hybrid working conditions: Work from home up to two days a week; home office budget (after the probation period); modern Danske Campus workplace developed with anthropologist for the best employee experience.
Additional days of leave: for rest, health, volunteering, exams in higher education institutions, and other important activities. Moreover, for seniority with Danske Bank.
Monetary compensation package: accidents & critical diseases insurance; financial support in case of unfortunate events, travel insurance; IIIrd Pillar Pension Fund contribution.


See all the benefits HERE.

Your job function (position) in the job contract will be Technology Business Solutions.

Have we caught your attention? Apply now!

We process applications continuously, so we encourage you to apply sooner rather than later.

For more information, please contact Hiring Managers Michael Friis Bak on mras@danskebank.dk or Interim Product Owner Freja Hallund Slot on frsl@danskebank.dk

About Us

Danske Bank is a Nordic bank with bridges to the world around us. For 150 years, we have supported people and businesses in releasing their potential. A career with us is an opportunity to join a community of 22,000 colleagues in a culture where we are committed to Teaming Up, Owning It and Being Open. Together, we are on a journey to transform Danske Bank into a better bank. For our customers, our employees and the societies around us.

Title: Graduate Program Data Science & Analytics - Denmark
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?


Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.


Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:


You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
You are fluent in English and Danish.
You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
You have the legal right to work in Denmark (e.g., residency, visa).
You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

HTML:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?

  • Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
  • Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
  • Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.

Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:

  • You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
  • You are fluent in English and Danish.
  • You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
  • You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
  • You have the legal right to work in Denmark (e.g., residency, visa).
  • You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
  • You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

Apply Now!

Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity. Apply today and discover what's in it for you!

Title: Ungarbejder - 7 timer - Farum
Place: Farum, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Introduktion

Er du under 18 år? Og drømmer du om at få et job, der giver lidt ekstra penge til dig selv hver måned? Så er du måske vores nye ungarbejder!

På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold. Og som ungarbejder i Lidl får du et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen.*

Mød din nye kollega Katrine

”Jeg er så glad for at arbejde i Lidl! Her er plads til alle, og alle bliver hørt. Vi har et rigtig godt team, hvor alle er gode til at hjælpe hinanden. Selve arbejdsmiljøet består af gode kunder, søde kollegaer og en flot butik,” fortæller Katrine, der er 17 år gammel og ungarbejder i Lidl.

Kan du bidrage til godt humør, fællesskab og en god service? Så er det måske dig, vi står og mangler!

På #teamlidl kommer du på hold med verdensklasse kollegaer. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter. Vi går nemlig op i, at du lærer tingene rigtigt fra start, at du lykkes i dit job, og at du bidrager til et stærkt sammenhold med dine kollegaer.

Grænseløse karrieremulighederSom ungarbejder er der mange forskellige udviklingsmuligheder hos os. Har du f.eks. ambitioner om at gøre karriere i dagligvarebranchen? Så er jobbet som ungarbejder det perfekte sted at starte. Din erfaring som ungarbejder vil nemlig kun være en fordel, hvis du senere ønsker at søge ind på vores elevuddannelse, som vi mener, er Danmarks suverænt bedste! Det er ligeledes en stor fordel at have butikserfaring, hvis du drømmer om at arbejde på vores hovedkontor.


Ift. den overenskomstmæssig takst pr. marts 2024.


Om jobbet

Som ungarbejder hos os skal du, først og fremmest, hjælpe dine kollegaer, så vores kunder altid får en god indkøbsoplevelse i butikken. Du ved, at du har gjort dit arbejde godt, når du har ydet en god service – og det gør dig glad! Dine opgaver er blandt andet at:


Give vores kunder en god oplevelse i butikken sammen med dine kollegaer
Få butikken til at shine, ved at fylde op og trimme hylderne så de altid står snorlige
Få varerne scannet hurtigt gennem kasseapparatet med et smil på læben
Svinge moppen, når gulvet er beskidt


Om dig

Når du starter i Lidl, får du en grundig oplæring i butikken. Derfor er det ikke afgørende for os, at du har erfaring, men vi forventer, at du:


Er under 18 år, dog ikke yngre end 15 år
Har afsluttet 9. klasse
Er smilende og har masser af god energi
Har gåpåmod, klør på og vægter teamspirit højt
Møder til tiden og er interesseret i at gøre dit arbejde godt
Har mulighed for at arbejde eftermiddag, aften og i weekenden


Vi tilbyder

Udover at du får verdensklasse kollegaer, får du som ungarbejder også:


Et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen*
Løn og vilkår efter gældende overenskomst med HK
Erfaring med at samarbejde i et team og erfaring med service - hvilket vil give dig en stor fordel i dit videre arbejdsliv
Fleksible arbejdstider, så du let kan kombinere jobbet med skole og fritidsaktiviteter
Et spændende og varieret job med fart over feltet!
Gode karrieremuligheder, hvor du får forudsætningerne for at lykkes
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.


Vil du med på #teamlidl?

Nu ved du forhåbentlig endnu mere om #teamlidl og jobbet som ungarbejder, end du gjorde i forvejen! Er jobbet som ungarbejder i Lidl noget for dig? Så send dit motiverede CV allerede i dag!

Sådan søger du stillingen:


Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV


Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på tlf. 7635 0635 på hverdage indenfor normal arbejdstid eller på jobilidloest@lidl.dk

Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!
