Title: Student Assistant - Data Activation (Enterprise Data, Enterprise Digital Enablement)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Want hands-on experience with modern data tools while helping a global organization become truly data-driven?

At Lundbeck, we are on a clear journey to becoming a bionic company, where data, AI, and digital capabilities are embedded in how we make decisions, move faster, and create value.

We are looking for a Student Assistant to join our Data Activation team within Enterprise Digital Enablement at Lundbeck.

This is a role where you will be actively involved from day one. You will work directly with real data, real stakeholders, and real use cases, helping turn data into something the business actually uses.

Your new role

You will be part of a team focused on making data usable, accessible, and meaningful across the organization.

Your work will include:


Supporting and helping run our internal community of data and BI practitioners, while creating visibility across initiatives, use cases, and data products.
Contributing to the development of data and analytics solutions using tools like Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, dbt, and Snowflake.
Helping make data governance practical, relevant, and easy to work with.
Collaborating with stakeholders across the business to support adoption of data and BI solutions.
Making data easier to understand and use through structure, communication, and hands-on support.



You will be working in the space between data engineering, BI, and business adoption, where data becomes valuable and actionable.

Your future team

You will become part of Enterprise Data within Enterprise Digital Enablement, a department that brings together Data Engineering, BI, and Data & BI adoption capabilities. The team plays a key role in enabling data to create value across Lundbeck.

You will join the Data Activation team in Enterprise Digital Enablement and work closely with colleagues across Enterprise Data and report to the Head of Data Activation.

Enterprise Data builds the foundation through data platforms, models, and products. Our role is to activate that foundation by ensuring it is adopted and used across the business.

We work with a modern data stack, including Snowflake, dbt, Power BI, and Microsoft Fabric, and collaborate closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders.

The environment is informal, collaborative, and supportive, with a strong focus on learning, growth, and making an impact.

The position is based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

What You Bring To The Team


You are studying a relevant field (e.g., Data Science, Computer Science, BI, Statistics, or similar).
You have basic skills in SQL, Python, R, or similar.
You are curious about how data is used in a business context, not just how it is built.
You are structured, proactive, and take ownership of your work.
You are comfortable communicating with different types of stakeholders.
Experience with Power BI or Snowflake is a plus—but not required.
You can work :20 hours/week and have 2–3 years remaining in your studies.
You are fluent in English.



Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion.

Apply now

Can you see yourself in this role? We want to hear from you. Does this sound like your dream job, but you’re not sure if you meet all the requirements? We still want to hear from you!

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

Applications must be received by April 30, 2026.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck).

#EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.

Want hands-on experience with modern data tools while helping a global organization become truly data-driven?

At Lundbeck, we are on a clear journey to becoming a bionic company, where data, AI, and digital capabilities are embedded in how we make decisions, move faster, and create value.

We are looking for a Student Assistant to join our Data Activation team within Enterprise Digital Enablement at Lundbeck.

This is a role where you will be actively involved from day one. You will work directly with real data, real stakeholders, and real use cases, helping turn data into something the business actually uses.

Your new role

You will be part of a team focused on making data usable, accessible, and meaningful across the organization.

Your work will include:

  • Supporting and helping run our internal community of data and BI practitioners, while creating visibility across initiatives, use cases, and data products.
  • Contributing to the development of data and analytics solutions using tools like Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, dbt, and Snowflake.
  • Helping make data governance practical, relevant, and easy to work with.
  • Collaborating with stakeholders across the business to support adoption of data and BI solutions.
  • Making data easier to understand and use through structure, communication, and hands-on support.


You will be working in the space between data engineering, BI, and business adoption, where data becomes valuable and actionable.

Your future team

You will become part of Enterprise Data within Enterprise Digital Enablement, a department that brings together Data Engineering, BI, and Data & BI adoption capabilities. The team plays a key role in enabling data to create value across Lundbeck.

You will join the Data Activation team in Enterprise Digital Enablement and work closely with colleagues across Enterprise Data and report to the Head of Data Activation.

Enterprise Data builds the foundation through data platforms, models, and products. Our role is to activate that foundation by ensuring it is adopted and used across the business.

We work with a modern data stack, including Snowflake, dbt, Power BI, and Microsoft Fabric, and collaborate closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders.

The environment is informal, collaborative, and supportive, with a strong focus on learning, growth, and making an impact.

The position is based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

What You Bring To The Team

  • You are studying a relevant field (e.g., Data Science, Computer Science, BI, Statistics, or similar).
  • You have basic skills in SQL, Python, R, or similar.
  • You are curious about how data is used in a business context, not just how it is built.
  • You are structured, proactive, and take ownership of your work.
  • You are comfortable communicating with different types of stakeholders.
  • Experience with Power BI or Snowflake is a plus—but not required.
  • You can work :20 hours/week and have 2–3 years remaining in your studies.
  • You are fluent in English.


Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion.

Apply now

Can you see yourself in this role? We want to hear from you. Does this sound like your dream job, but you’re not sure if you meet all the requirements? We still want to hear from you!

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

Applications must be received by April 30, 2026.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck).

#EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.

Title: Grocery Associate (part-time)
Place: Søborg, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Wolt

At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.

Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.

Calling all team-players - are you looking for a flexible role that could shape the future of retail? If so, then let’s talk!

We are looking for a part-time (10-15 hours per week - morning shifts) Grocery Associate to join our Wolt Market team in Søborg! 🤩

What You’ll Be Doing


Taking in orders through our Merchant app
Picking and packing groceries, and handing them to customers and courier partners
Unloading food and supplies that we receive from vendors
Managing our internal systems which include precise information about nutrients and allergens
Maintaining order and cleanliness of the store


Our humble expectations


You are proactive and a fast learner with the ability to multitask
Strict requirement is that you can work morning shifts between 07:00-15:00 o'clock both on weekdays and weekends
You're able to commute to the Wolt Market in early mornings - the store address is Telefonvej 8, 2860 Søborg
Experience from working in a grocery store is an advantage!
Fluent English is required, Danish is a plus
You have a valid right to work in Denmark and a Danish CPR number


What You’ll Get By Joining Us


Wolt credits cashback benefit
Employee assistance program
Wolt-wide training programs


If you are excited about working in a high-growth environment, taking ownership, and being part of an extremely ambitious team, then click below to apply and get the conversation going!

We will be reviewing applications on an on-going basis, so send through your application along with your CV as soon as possible! 💙

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

Title: Commercial Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Commercial Analyst

Location: Amsterdam or Copenhagen or Brussels

About Us

With over 200 brands sold in nearly 180 countries, we’re the world’s leading premium drinks company. From global icons like Johnnie Walker, Guinness and Tanqueray to Smirnoff and Don Julio, we create brands consumers love. Bring your passion and use your curiosity as you explore, collaborate and innovate.

Together with passionate people from all over the world, you’ll test new ideas, learn and grow, and unlock a brighter, more exciting future.

Join us to create a career worth celebrating.

About The Function

Our Sales team love building relationships, connecting with customers to sell our much-loved brands in new and vibrant markets where they can thrive. They help sell our iconic brands across 180 countries every year.

We’re operating across different markets, channels, and areas of expertise. Using your curiosity and passion, you’ll make the most of consumer insight and digital platforms, reaching new customers, markets, and celebrations to help us achieve our growth potential. We’ll support your learning and growth as you develop your career with us and work with people across our business to realise your fullest potential.

About The Role

In this role, you will act as a key analytics and insight partner to the Commercial and Field Sales teams, enabling better decision‑making through best‑in‑class reporting and data‑driven insights. The Commercial Analyst owns the development of robust, scalable performance reporting and translates complex data into clear commercial stories that drive execution and impact at the point of purchase.

This role combines strong technical capability (Power BI, data modelling) with a commercial mindset, ensuring analytics are tightly linked to business priorities, performance improvement, and growth opportunities.

Role Responsibilities

Commercial Insight & Business Partnering


Act as a data‑driven sparring partner for Commercial and Field Sales Leaders, shaping performance discussions through clear insights and recommendations.
Translate commercial strategy into measurable KPIs, dashboards, and performance tracking frameworks.
Proactively identify trends, risks, and opportunities in sales, distribution, pricing, and execution performance.
Support leadership decision‑making by turning analysis into practical, action‑oriented commercial guidance.


Reporting & Analytics Excellence


Design, build, and maintain end‑to‑end Power BI reporting solutions, including data models, DAX measures, and intuitive dashboard experiences tailored to business users.
Own the monthly market and performance reporting cycle, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and relevance across Benelux & Nordics.
Partner with the Analytics and Technology teams to continuously improve data pipelines, metrics definitions, and reporting standards.


Performance Monitoring & Decision Support


Deliver monthly performance deep‑dives highlighting execution quality, KPI progress, and root causes of under‑ or over‑performance.
Provide analytical support for Monthly Business Reviews, Demand Reviews, and ad‑hoc commercial questions.
Support target setting, forecasting, and incentive tracking with robust, traceable data and clear performance logic.
Ensure insights are clearly communicated to non‑technical audiences through compelling narratives and visuals.


Systems & Data Governance


Work closely with Technology Product Owners and data leads to ensure reporting tools and data sources remain fit‑for‑purpose and aligned with business needs.
Monitor data quality, metric consistency, and system changes impacting commercial reporting.
Support adoption of reporting tools by end users through training, documentation, and continuous improvement.


About You


5+ years of experience in commercial analytics, business intelligence, or sales performance roles, preferably within FMCG.
Advanced Power BI expertise (data modelling, DAX, performance optimization, UX principles).
Strong commercial acumen with the ability to connect data insights to revenue, margin, and execution outcomes.
Experience presenting complex analysis clearly and confidently to senior commercial stakeholders.
Curious, structured thinker with a strong drive to improve how data supports business decisions.
Python skills are a nice to have, but not mandatory.


Flexible Working Statement

Flexibility is key to our success. Talk to us about what flexibility means to you, so that you’re supported to manage your wellbeing and balance your priorities from day one.

Rewards & Benefits Statement

We recognise and value performance, offering our people a highly competitive Rewards and Benefits package including:

Diversity statement

Our purpose is to celebrate life, every day, everywhere. And creating an inclusive culture, where everyone feels valued and that they can belong, is a crucial part of this.

We embrace diversity in the broadest possible sense. This means that you’ll be welcomed and celebrated for who you are just by being you. You’ll be part of and help build and champion an inclusive culture that celebrates people of different gender, ethnicity, ability, age, sexual orientation, social class, educational backgrounds, experiences, mindsets, and more.

Our ambition is to create the best performing, most trusted and respected consumer products companies in the world. Join us and help transform our business as we take our brands to new heights and build new ones as part of shaping the next generation of celebrations for consumers around the world.

Feel inspired? Then this may be the opportunity for you.

If you require a reasonable adjustment, please ensure that you capture this information when you submit your application.

Recruitment Scam Warning

Protecting candidates is very important to us. All communications regarding your application will come from an email address ending in @diageo.com. In our recruitment process, we'll never ask for money.

Worker Type

Regular

Primary Location:

Amsterdam

Additional Locations :

Brussels Center, Copenhagen

Job Posting Start Date

2026-04-08
